examples.en: Don’t scowl—tell me what’s wrong.
examples.zh: 别板着脸——告诉我怎么了。

etymology: 源自古英语/中古英语中表示“皱眉、怒视”的动词形式，含义长期稳定，核心就是“以皱眉表达不满或愤怒”。
mnemonic: 把它拆开记：s + cowl（兜帽）→ 皱眉时眉眼像被“兜帽”罩住，脸色很阴沉。
联想中文“臭脸/黑脸”：看到 scowl 就想到不高兴的表情。

summary.en: With an angry, disapproving look.
summary.zh: 带着愤怒或不满表情地。

definition.en: In a scowling way; with an angry or disapproving facial expression.
definition.zh: 怒视地；板着脸地；带着不满表情地。

examples.en: He looked at me scowlingly and said nothing.
examples.zh: 他板着脸怒视着我，一句话也没说。

examples.en: She scowlingly pushed past the crowd to the front.
examples.zh: 她一脸不快地挤过人群走到前面。

examples.en: The customer scowlingly complained about the long wait.
examples.zh: 顾客皱着眉头、很不高兴地抱怨等得太久。

etymology: 由动词 scowl（“皱眉怒视”）+ 副词后缀 -ingly 构成，表示“以……的方式”。scowl 来自中古英语，含“皱眉、愠怒地看”的意思。
mnemonic: 把它拆开记：scowl（皱眉怒视）+ -ingly（……地）= “皱眉怒视地”。
联想画面：别人“scowl”时脸很“凶”，所以做事/说话也会是“凶巴巴地”。

summary.en: An angry or disapproving look; to show it with your face.
summary.zh: 愤怒或不满的表情；用表情表示怒视。

definition.en: A facial expression showing anger, disapproval, or deep annoyance (brows pulled together, mouth turned down).
definition.zh: 怒视的表情；皱眉、嘴角下撇，表示愤怒、不满或烦躁。

examples.en: His scowls made the room go quiet.
examples.zh: 他一脸怒容，房间里立刻安静下来。

examples.en: She answered with a scowl instead of words.
examples.zh: 她没有说话，只用一个怒视的表情回应。

definition.en: To look at someone or something with anger or disapproval.
definition.zh: 怒视；皱眉瞪视，表示愤怒或不赞同。

examples.en: He scowled at the rude customer.
examples.zh: 他愤怒地瞪着那个无礼的顾客。

examples.en: Don’t scowl—tell me what’s wrong.
examples.zh: 别皱着眉头了，告诉我哪里不对。

examples.en: She scowled when she heard the news.
examples.zh: 她听到消息时皱起眉头，脸色很难看。

extraExplanation: “scowls”通常是 scowl 的复数名词（many scowls）或第三人称单数动词（he/she scowls）。例如：He scowls when he’s stressed.（他压力大时会皱眉。）
etymology: 来自中古英语，原意与“皱眉、板起脸”有关；后来固定为表示愤怒或不满的表情/动作。
mnemonic: 把 scowl 想成“s + cowl（兜帽）”：把脸“罩”起来，眉头一皱，表情变冷。
联想中文“皱（zhou）”的感觉：scowl 就是“皱眉瞪人”的那种表情。

summary.en: looking like you are scowling; unfriendly-looking
summary.zh: 皱眉不悦的神态；看起来不友好

definition.en: Having a scowl; looking angry or unfriendly (informal).
definition.zh: 皱眉怒视的；看起来生气或不友好的（非正式）。

examples.en: He gave me a scowly look when I asked about the deadline.
examples.zh: 我问截止日期时，他给了我一个皱眉的不友好眼神。

examples.en: The cashier seemed scowly, but she was just tired.
examples.zh: 收银员看起来有点皱眉不悦，其实她只是累了。

extraExplanation: “scowly”比“angry”更强调“表情/神态”，不一定真的生气；有时只是看起来凶或不耐烦。
更正式写法通常用“scowling”或“with a scowl”。
etymology: 由“scowl（皱眉怒视）+ 形容词后缀 -y”构成，表示“带有……样子的/有……特征的”。常见于口语和轻松语境。
mnemonic: 把它拆开记：scowl（皱眉）+ y（像……的）→“皱眉样的”。
联想中文“皱着眉头”的“皱”= scowl，看到“scowly”就想到“皱眉脸”。

summary.en: an electronic power-control switch (thyristor)
summary.zh: 用于电力控制的电子开关（晶闸管）

definition.en: a silicon-controlled rectifier; an electronic switch (a type of thyristor) used to control power in circuits
definition.zh: 可控硅整流器；一种用于电路中控制电能的电子开关（属于晶闸管的一种）

examples.en: The engineer replaced the SCR to fix the power control problem.
examples.zh: 工程师更换了可控硅（SCR），以修复电源控制问题。

examples.en: An SCR can regulate how much power reaches the motor.
examples.zh: 可控硅（SCR）可以调节到达电机的功率大小。

extraExplanation: 在工程语境里，SCR常被直接读作字母：S-C-R。它常见于调光器、马达调速、电源控制等电路中。
不同领域里SCR也可能代表其他短语（如机构名、项目名等）。如果你告诉我具体上下文（电子、电路、聊天、公司名等），我可以补充对应释义。
etymology: 缩写词，来自 silicon-controlled rectifier（“硅控制整流器”）。该器件在20世纪中期电力电子发展中广泛使用。
mnemonic: 把它记成“Si（硅） + Controlled（可控） + Rectifier（整流器）”，三个关键词刚好对应S-C-R。
联想“可控硅=可控制电流的开关”，SCR就是这种“可控的整流开关”。

summary.en: “scr” is usually an informal abbreviation; in tech chat it may mean “screenshot,” while in engineering “SCR” commonly means “silicon-controlled rectifier.”
summary.zh: “scr”通常是非正式缩写；在技术聊天中可能指“截图”，而在工程语境中大写“SCR”常指“硅控整流器”。

definition.en: An abbreviation used in casual writing (especially online) that can stand for different things depending on context, such as “screen/screenshot,” “scratch,” or other shortened forms.
definition.zh: 一种在非正式写作（尤其是网络场景）中使用的缩写，具体含义取决于语境，可能代表“screen/screenshot（屏幕/截图）”“scratch（抓/划）”等其他缩写形式。

examples.en: Can you send an scr of the error message?
examples.zh: 你能发一张错误信息的截图（scr）吗？

examples.en: I only saw “scr” in the chat—what does it mean here?
examples.zh: 我只在聊天里看到“scr”——这里它是什么意思？

definition.en: SCR: “silicon-controlled rectifier,” an electronic component used to control electrical power (common in engineering contexts).
definition.zh: SCR：“silicon-controlled rectifier（硅控整流器）”，一种用于控制电功率的电子元件（常见于工程语境）。

examples.en: The circuit uses an SCR to regulate the current.
examples.zh: 这个电路使用SCR（硅控整流器）来调节电流。

extraExplanation: “scr”本身不是一个固定的标准英语单词，更常见的是口语/网络中的随手缩写。要准确理解，最好看上下文：如果出现在聊天、报错、教程里，常常是在说“截图（screenshot）”；如果在电子工程、电路资料里，大写“SCR”几乎就是“硅控整流器”。
写作中如果面对不熟悉的人或正式场合，建议直接写全：screenshot / screen 或 SCR (silicon-controlled rectifier)，避免误解。

summary.en: Scrabble is a word-building board game played with letter tiles to score points.
summary.zh: Scrabble 是用字母牌在棋盘上拼单词并得分的桌上游戏。

definition.en: a board game where players make words by placing letter tiles on a grid to score points
definition.zh: 一种桌上拼字游戏：玩家在棋盘格子上摆放字母牌组成单词来得分

examples.en: Do you want to play Scrabble after dinner?
examples.zh: 晚饭后想玩一局拼字游戏（Scrabble）吗？

examples.en: She’s great at Scrabble because she knows many words.
examples.zh: 她很擅长Scrabble，因为她词汇量很大。

extraExplanation: Scrabble 是专有名词（品牌名），常写作大写 Scrabble。口语里有时也会泛指同类“拼字游戏”。
玩法文化点：英语国家里 Scrabble 常被当作“词汇与拼写能力”的休闲挑战，也常出现在家庭聚会、图书馆活动或校园社团中。
相关表达：play Scrabble（玩Scrabble）；a Scrabble board/tiles（棋盘/字母牌）；a Scrabble word（在Scrabble里可用的单词）。

summary.en: Scrabble is a popular word tile board game; as a verb, scrabble can mean to scratch or rummage around quickly.
summary.zh: Scrabble 是流行的拼字棋盘游戏；作动词时 scrabble 可表示“抓挠”或“慌乱地翻找”。

definition.en: a word board game in which players make words from letter tiles on a grid, earning points based on letter values and board bonuses
definition.zh: 一种拼字棋盘游戏：玩家用字母牌在方格棋盘上组成单词，并根据字母分值和棋盘加成计分

examples.en: We played Scrabble after dinner.
examples.zh: 晚饭后我们玩了拼字游戏（Scrabble）。

examples.en: She scored 42 points with the word “QUIZ” in Scrabble.
examples.zh: 她在Scrabble里用“QUIZ”这个词拿了42分。

examples.en: Scrabble is a fun way to practice English vocabulary.
examples.zh: Scrabble是练习英语词汇的有趣方式。

definition.en: to scratch or claw at something; to move or work with quick, small, rough motions (less common)
definition.zh: 抓挠；用爪子或手快速而粗略地乱动（较不常见）

examples.en: The cat scrabbled at the door to be let in.
examples.zh: 猫抓挠着门想进来。

examples.en: He scrabbled in his bag for his keys.
examples.zh: 他在包里慌乱地摸索找钥匙。

extraExplanation: Scrabble 是一个非常知名的游戏名（品牌名），写作首字母大写时常指“正版/特定版本”的拼字游戏；小写 scrabble 有时也被泛用来表示“玩拼字游戏”。
词源上，scrabble 这个动词原义与“抓挠、乱翻、费力摸索”有关；游戏名借用了这种“到处翻找字母、拼出单词”的感觉。

summary.en: hurried, awkward scratching or desperate grabbing for something
summary.zh: 慌乱笨拙地抓挠或拼命争抢

definition.en: scratching, clawing, or moving with quick, awkward hands or feet, often on a rough surface
definition.zh: （用手脚）快速笨拙地抓、刨、扒；常指在粗糙表面上乱抓乱爬

examples.en: The cat went scrabbling at the door to be let in.
examples.zh: 猫在门上乱抓，想让人放它进来。

examples.en: He was scrabbling up the rocky slope on hands and knees.
examples.zh: 他手脚并用，在岩石斜坡上吃力地往上爬。

definition.en: trying hard to get something (especially money, power, or attention) in a hurried, competitive, or desperate way
definition.zh: （为得到某物，尤指钱、权力或关注）忙乱地争抢、拼命地追求

examples.en: People were scrabbling for the last tickets.
examples.zh: 人们在争抢最后几张票。

examples.en: After the company failed, he spent months scrabbling for work.
examples.zh: 公司倒闭后，他有好几个月都在四处奔波找工作。

definition.en: showing hurried, desperate effort to get or keep something
definition.zh: （行为或状态）忙乱的、拼命的、带点绝望的

examples.en: She made a scrabbling attempt to save the papers from the wind.
examples.zh: 她慌忙去抢救快被风吹走的文件。

examples.en: There was a scrabbling struggle for control of the microphone.
examples.zh: 大家为抢麦克风一阵乱抢。

definition.en: the act of scrabbling; frantic scratching or grabbing
definition.zh: 乱抓乱刨（的动作）；慌乱的争抢

examples.en: We heard scrabbling under the floorboards.
examples.zh: 我们听到地板下面有乱抓乱刨的声音。

examples.en: In the dark, there was scrabbling for the flashlight.
examples.zh: 在黑暗中，大家慌乱地摸索抢手电筒。

etymology: 来自英语动词 scrabble（乱抓、乱翻、急忙地找），可能与表示“抓挠/刨”的日耳曼语词源有关；scrabbling 是其现在分词形式，也常作形容词或名词使用。
mnemonic: 联想“scrape（刮）+ scramble（慌乱爬/抢）”：又刮又乱，手脚并用地“乱抓乱爬/乱抢”。
想成中文“抓（zhuā）+扒（bā）”的画面：桌上东西要掉了，你急得一直抓扒。

summary.en: “scracian” is not a recognized English word; it’s likely a misspelling or a context-specific proper name.
summary.zh: “scracian”不是常见英语词，更可能是拼写错误或特定语境中的专有名词。

definition.en: Not a standard English word. It is most likely a misspelling or a name/term from a specific context (e.g., a username, fantasy word, or a proper noun).
definition.zh: 不是标准英语单词。更可能是拼写错误，或来自特定语境的名称/术语（例如用户名、奇幻作品用词或专有名词）。

examples.en: I can’t find “scracian” in standard dictionaries—did you mean “Socratic” or “Thracian”?
examples.zh: 我在常见词典里找不到“scracian”——你是不是想写“Socratic（苏格拉底式的）”或“Thracian（色雷斯的）”？

examples.en: If “scracian” is a character or place name, please share the sentence so I can explain it accurately.
examples.zh: 如果“scracian”是角色名或地名，请把完整句子发我，我才能更准确地解释。

extraExplanation:
“scracian”很像拼写误差。最常见、也最接近的可能词：
1) Socratic /səˈkrætɪk/：苏格拉底的；常见于“the Socratic method（苏格拉底式问答法）”。
2) Thracian /ˈθreɪʃən/：色雷斯的（古代地区/民族）。
3) Arcadian /ɑrˈkeɪdiən/：阿卡迪亚的；常用来表示“田园诗般的、理想化乡村的”。
把你看到“scracian”的原句或出处发来，我可以帮你锁定正确词并做详细讲解。

summary.en: Scraggly means thin and messy-looking, often because it’s poorly cared for or not healthy.
summary.zh: Scraggly 指“稀疏又凌乱、看起来缺乏照料或不太健康”的外观。

definition.en: (Of a person, animal, plant, or hair) thin, untidy, and not in good condition; looking rough or poorly cared for.
definition.zh: （指人、动物、植物或头发）稀疏凌乱、状态不佳的；看起来粗糙、缺乏照料的。

examples.en: A scraggly dog stood by the road, looking hungry.
examples.zh: 一只毛发稀疏凌乱的狗站在路边，看起来很饿。

examples.en: He had a scraggly beard that made him look older.
examples.zh: 他留着一把稀疏又乱的胡子，让他看起来更老。

examples.en: A few scraggly bushes grew in the dry soil.
examples.zh: 干燥的土里长着几丛稀稀拉拉的灌木。

extraExplanation: “scraggly”常带有一点同情或轻微贬义，强调“稀疏 + 凌乱 + 不健康/不体面”的感觉。可用来形容胡子（scraggly beard）、头发（scraggly hair）、植物（scraggly trees/bushes）或动物外观。
近义词语感对比：shaggy 更偏“毛长而乱”；messy 只是“乱”；unkempt 偏“没打理”；scrawny 更偏“瘦得可怜/干瘦”（多形容人或动物身形）。

summary.en: “Scraggy” describes something that looks thin, bony, and rough or unhealthy (often hair, animals, or plants).
summary.zh: “scraggy”形容看起来又瘦又骨感、粗糙或不健康的状态（常用于头发、动物或植物）。

definition.en: Thin, bony, and not attractive; with a rough or unhealthy look.
definition.zh: 瘦骨嶙峋的；不太好看、显得粗糙或不健康的。

examples.en: A scraggy dog followed us down the street.
examples.zh: 一只瘦骨嶙峋的狗跟着我们沿街走。

examples.en: He had scraggy hair that stuck out in every direction.
examples.zh: 他的头发又乱又稀疏，向四面八方翘着。

definition.en: Small and thin in a way that seems weak or underdeveloped (often used about plants or young animals).
definition.zh: （多指植物或幼小动物）细小瘦弱、长得不壮实的。

examples.en: Only a few scraggy trees grew on the windy hill.
examples.zh: 风大的山坡上只长着几棵瘦弱的小树。

examples.en: The garden looked scraggy after weeks without rain.
examples.zh: 几周没下雨后，花园看起来枯瘦稀疏。

extraExplanation: “scraggy”带有明显的负面色彩，常用来形容外表“又瘦又乱、显得不健康或不体面”。用于人时可能显得不礼貌；描述动物、树木、头发等更常见、更自然。
词源上与“scrag”（瘦长的脖子/瘦骨）有关，所以常让人联想到“骨头突出、干瘪”。
近义词语气差异：skinny（偏“瘦”，不一定难看）、scrawny（瘦弱/营养不良感更强）、ragged（破旧凌乱，侧重“破”而非“瘦”）。

summary.en: “Scram” is an informal way to say “leave quickly,” and it can also be a rude command meaning “go away!”
summary.zh: “scram”是口语，表示“赶紧离开”，也可作不太礼貌的命令，意思是“走开！”

definition.en: (Informal) To leave quickly, often because you are told to or because of trouble.
definition.zh: （非正式）赶紧离开；快走（常因被赶走或遇到麻烦）。

examples.en: Scram—I don’t want any trouble.
examples.zh: 快走——我不想惹麻烦。

examples.en: When the police arrived, the kids scrammed.
examples.zh: 警察一来，孩子们就溜走了。

definition.en: A command telling someone to go away immediately: “Scram!”
definition.zh: （命令）“走开！”“快滚！”（让人立刻离开）。

examples.en: He pointed at the door and shouted, “Scram!”
examples.zh: 他指着门大喊：“走开！”

examples.en: The sign basically says, “Scram!” to trespassers.
examples.zh: 那块牌子基本上是在对擅闯者说：“走开！”

extraExplanation: “scram”很口语，语气通常粗鲁或不耐烦；对陌生人直接说可能显得不礼貌。更中性的说法有：“Please leave.”、“Go away.”或“Could you step outside?”
词源常被认为来自20世纪初的俚语，可能与“scamper（急跑）”有关。二战后在口语中更常见。
也常见于固定搭配："scram out of here"（从这儿赶紧走）与 "scram!"（走开！）。

summary.en: able to be mixed up or rearranged
summary.zh: 可被打乱或重新排列

definition.en: Able to be scrambled (mixed up, rearranged, or jumbled).
definition.zh: 可被打乱的；可被重新排列的；可被搅乱的。

examples.en: The letters are scramblable, so you can rearrange them to form many words.
examples.zh: 这些字母是可被打乱重排的，所以你可以重新排列它们组成很多单词。

examples.en: Make sure the quiz questions are scramblable so each student gets a different order.
examples.zh: 要确保测验题目是可打乱顺序的，这样每个学生拿到的顺序都不同。

extraExplanation: 这个词在日常口语里不算常见，更常见的表达是 “can be scrambled” 或 “scramble-able”。在游戏、教学软件、密码/字母重排等语境中更容易见到。
常见搭配：scramblable letters/words/questions/order（可打乱的字母/单词/题目/顺序）。
etymology: 由 scramble（打乱、搅乱、乱排）+ 形容词后缀 -able（“能够被……”）构成，表示“能够被打乱/重排”。
mnemonic: 记 -able = “可以被……的”：readable（可读的）→ scramblable（可被打乱的）。
scramble 有“炒蛋（scrambled eggs，把蛋打散）”的画面感：能被打散、搅乱的就是 scramblable。

summary.en: Scramble means to mix things in a messy way, or to move quickly using hands and feet; as a noun it can mean a rushed, confused struggle for something.
summary.zh: scramble可指把东西打乱/搅乱，也可指手脚并用地匆忙攀爬或奔走；作名词时可表示混乱中的争抢。

definition.en: to mix things (often words, letters, or food) in a messy or random way
definition.zh: 把东西（常指字母、单词或食物）胡乱/随机地搅在一起

examples.en: Scramble the eggs with a fork, then cook them in a pan.
examples.zh: 用叉子把鸡蛋打散，然后在平底锅里炒。

examples.en: The teacher scrambled the letters and asked us to form a word.
examples.zh: 老师把字母打乱，让我们拼出一个单词。

definition.en: to move quickly and awkwardly, using hands and feet (often to climb or to get somewhere fast)
definition.zh: 手脚并用地快速攀爬或匆忙前进（动作有点狼狈）

examples.en: We scrambled up the hill before it got dark.
examples.zh: 天黑前我们手脚并用地爬上了山坡。

examples.en: When the alarm rang, everyone scrambled to the exits.
examples.zh: 警报一响，大家都慌忙冲向出口。

definition.en: a confused or disordered situation; a hurried effort to get something (often: a scramble for something)
definition.zh: 混乱的局面；争抢/抢夺（常用：a scramble for ...）

examples.en: There was a scramble for seats when the doors opened.
examples.zh: 门一开，大家就争抢座位。

examples.en: In the scramble to finish, he made several mistakes.
examples.zh: 在赶着完成的忙乱中，他犯了好几个错误。

extraExplanation: “scramble”在日常里很常见的搭配是：scrambled eggs（炒蛋/炒鸡蛋）和 scramble the letters（打乱字母）。
名词结构“a scramble for + 名词”常用来表示“对……的争抢/抢购”，比如 a scramble for tickets（抢票）。
文学与新闻写作里也常用“people scrambled”来营造紧张感：强调动作快、场面乱（比如灾难、警报、突发事件）。

summary.en: compete or rush eagerly to get something limited
summary.zh: 争先恐后地争取有限之物

definition.en: to compete in a hurried, eager way to get or achieve something (often because it is limited or valuable)
definition.zh: 争先恐后地抢夺/争取（通常因数量有限或很有价值）

examples.en: Fans scrambled for tickets as soon as the concert was announced.
examples.zh: 演唱会一公布，粉丝们就争先恐后地抢票。

examples.en: After the company posted the job, hundreds of applicants scrambled for interviews.
examples.zh: 公司发布职位后，数百名求职者争着获得面试机会。

examples.en: When the sale started, shoppers scrambled for the last few items.
examples.zh: 促销一开始，顾客们就抢购最后几件商品。

definition.en: to move quickly and awkwardly toward something, using hands and feet if necessary, in order to reach it first
definition.zh: （为先到达某处）手脚并用、急急忙忙地攀爬/奔跑

examples.en: The children scrambled for the best seats near the window.
examples.zh: 孩子们急忙抢到靠窗的最好座位。

examples.en: Hikers scrambled for higher ground when the water started rising.
examples.zh: 水位开始上涨时，徒步者们赶紧往高处攀爬。

etymology: “scramble”原指“手脚并用地爬、乱爬”，后来引申为“混乱中争抢”，与介词“for”连用表示“为某物而争抢/争取”。
mnemonic: 联想“scramble=乱成一团/手忙脚乱”，scramble for 就是“手忙脚乱地去抢（for 后面的东西）”。
把它和中文“抢着要/争着要”对应记：scramble for tickets=抢票。

summary.en: Scrambled means mixed up or out of order; it can also describe eggs cooked by stirring (scrambled eggs).
summary.zh: Scrambled 表示“被弄乱/顺序被打乱的”；也可指“炒蛋的”（scrambled eggs）。

definition.en: mixed up in a confused, messy, or disordered way (ideas, information, words, etc.).
definition.zh: （想法、信息、文字等）被弄乱的；混乱的；杂乱无章的。

examples.en: My notes are scrambled, so I can’t find the main points.
examples.zh: 我的笔记被弄乱了，所以我找不到要点。

examples.en: The directions were scrambled and hard to follow.
examples.zh: 说明被弄得很乱，很难照着做。

definition.en: to mix up or mess up; to put in the wrong order (often in passive: “be scrambled”).
definition.zh: 把……弄乱；把……打乱顺序（常用被动：be scrambled）。

examples.en: The pages got scrambled in my backpack.
examples.zh: 这些页在我背包里被弄乱了。

examples.en: The signal was scrambled, so we couldn’t hear clearly.
examples.zh: 信号被加扰了，所以我们听不清。

definition.en: cooked by stirring eggs while heating them (as in “scrambled eggs”).
definition.zh: （鸡蛋）炒的；炒蛋的（如 scrambled eggs）。

examples.en: I usually eat scrambled eggs for breakfast.
examples.zh: 我早餐通常吃炒蛋。

examples.en: She made scrambled eggs with cheese.
examples.zh: 她做了加芝士的炒蛋。

extraExplanation:
“scrambled”最常见的三种用法：
1) 形容“被弄乱/混乱的”（信息、想法、顺序等）；
2) 动词 scramble 的过去式/过去分词，表示“把……打乱/弄乱”（如 pages got scrambled）；
3) 固定搭配 “scrambled eggs” 指“炒蛋”。

在科技语境里，“scrambled signal/channel”常指“加扰/加密式干扰”，让内容不容易被直接接收或理解。

summary.en: Beaten eggs cooked while being stirred into soft curds.
summary.zh: 把鸡蛋打散并搅拌加热做成松软小块的炒蛋。

definition.en: Eggs beaten (often with a little milk, butter, salt, etc.) and cooked while stirring to form soft curds.
definition.zh: （常加少量牛奶、黄油、盐等）把鸡蛋打散后边搅拌边加热煎/炒成松软小块的做法。

examples.en: I usually have scrambled eggs for breakfast.
examples.zh: 我早餐通常吃炒蛋。

examples.en: Cook the scrambled eggs on low heat so they stay soft.
examples.zh: 用小火做炒蛋，这样口感更嫩。

examples.en: She made scrambled eggs with cheese and chives.
examples.zh: 她做了加芝士和细香葱的炒蛋。

definition.en: A serving or dish of scrambled eggs.
definition.zh: 一份炒蛋（这道菜/一盘炒蛋）。

examples.en: Can I get scrambled eggs and toast, please?
examples.zh: 请给我来一份炒蛋和吐司，好吗？

examples.en: The menu lists scrambled eggs with smoked salmon.
examples.zh: 菜单上有“烟熏三文鱼配炒蛋”。

extraExplanation: 英式英语也常说“scrambled egg(s)”。在餐厅点餐时更常用复数“scrambled eggs”，因为通常是一份由多个鸡蛋做成的。
口语里也会把“scrambled eggs”当作一种典型早餐意象，出现在美剧、电影或家庭对话中。
etymology: “scramble”原意有“匆忙、搅乱”的意思；在烹饪语境中引申为“把蛋打散并不断搅拌着煎熟”。“scrambled eggs”作为固定搭配很常见。
mnemonic: 把“scramble”记成“搅（乱）”：做这道菜的关键动作就是不断搅拌，让蛋液变成一小块一小块。

summary.en: Beaten eggs cooked while stirring.
summary.zh: 把鸡蛋打散后边搅拌边加热的炒蛋。

definition.en: Eggs beaten and cooked while being stirred, forming soft curds (a common breakfast dish).
definition.zh: 炒蛋；把鸡蛋打散后边搅拌边加热，形成软凝块的一种常见早餐。

examples.en: I usually eat scrambled eggs and toast for breakfast.
examples.zh: 我早餐通常吃炒蛋配吐司。

examples.en: Cook the scrambled eggs on low heat so they stay soft.
examples.zh: 用小火炒蛋，这样口感会更嫩。

examples.en: She added cheese and chives to her scrambled eggs.
examples.zh: 她在炒蛋里加了奶酪和细香葱。

extraExplanation: 美式英语里说“scrambled eggs”通常就是一盘炒蛋；口感可从“soft/creamy（更嫩更湿润）”到“firm/dry（更老更干）”。
常见搭配：with toast（配吐司）、with bacon/sausage（配培根/香肠）、season with salt and pepper（用盐和胡椒调味）。
etymology: “Scramble”原意有“匆忙/乱动/搅乱”，用于做菜时表示“把鸡蛋搅散并翻炒”。作为菜名，“scrambled eggs”在英语里很早就常见，用来指这种做法的鸡蛋。
mnemonic: 把“scramble”联想成“搅乱/搅拌”：把蛋液“搅得乱乱的”再炒，就是炒蛋。
“scrambled（被打乱的）+ eggs（鸡蛋）”=“被搅散的鸡蛋”。

summary.en: A “scrambler” is most commonly a device that makes a message or signal hard to understand; it can also mean a running-style quarterback, a cooking tool for eggs, or a rough-road motorcycle style.
summary.zh: “scrambler”最常指让信息/信号难以理解的“扰频/编码装置”；也可指跑动型四分卫、打蛋搅拌工具，或一种偏越野风格摩托车。

definition.en: A device or system that makes information (like speech, video, or data) hard to understand by mixing or encoding it, especially for privacy or security.
definition.zh: （尤指为隐私或安全）把语音、视频或数据“打乱/编码”从而难以理解的装置或系统（如加密电话设备）。

examples.en: The journalist used a phone scrambler to protect her sources.
examples.zh: 这位记者用电话加密器来保护消息来源。

examples.en: Modern apps replaced old voice scramblers with digital encryption.
examples.zh: 现代应用用数字加密取代了旧式语音扰频器。

definition.en: In American football, a quarterback who often runs to avoid defenders and gain yards instead of only throwing the ball.
definition.zh: （美式橄榄球）“善于躲闪奔跑推进”的四分卫（不只传球，也常跑动摆脱防守）。

examples.en: He’s a scrambler who can turn a broken play into a first down.
examples.zh: 他是个擅长摆脱的跑动型四分卫，能把失败的战术变成首攻。

examples.en: The defense struggled against the scrambler’s quick feet.
examples.zh: 防守方很难应付这名跑动型四分卫的灵活脚步。

definition.en: A tool used to stir and break up eggs (or other ingredients) while cooking.
definition.zh: （烹饪）打蛋器；搅散鸡蛋等食材的工具。

examples.en: She grabbed an egg scrambler and whisked the eggs in the pan.
examples.zh: 她拿起打蛋器，在锅里把鸡蛋搅散。

examples.en: A simple scrambler works fine for making fluffy scrambled eggs.
examples.zh: 做松软的炒蛋，用简单的打蛋工具就够了。

definition.en: A type of motorcycle designed for rough roads, mixing street and off-road styles (often called a “scrambler bike”).
definition.zh: 一种偏越野风格的摩托车（兼顾公路与非铺装路面，常称“scrambler”）。

examples.en: He rides a scrambler that handles gravel roads well.
examples.zh: 他骑的那辆scrambler很适合走碎石路。

examples.en: Scramblers became popular for their classic look and versatility.
examples.zh: scrambler因复古外形和多用途而流行。

extraExplanation: “scrambler”来自动词“scramble”（把……搅乱/打乱）。在科技语境中，早期“voice scrambler”多指把语音频段打乱的设备；今天更常用更强的概念是“encryption（加密）”。
体育含义主要是北美语境（美式橄榄球）。如果在其他运动或国家的英语环境里，可能需要补充说明“a scrambling quarterback”（会“乱中求进”的四分卫）。

summary.en: devices or people that scramble signals, data, or movement; also tools for scrambling eggs
summary.zh: 指加扰设备/程序、匆忙攀爬或乱跑的人，也可指炒蛋用的搅拌工具

definition.en: a device or software that mixes up (encodes) signals, messages, or data so others can’t understand it without decoding
definition.zh: （对信号、信息或数据进行扰乱/编码的）加扰器；扰码器

examples.en: The channel used a scrambler to protect the broadcast signal.
examples.zh: 该频道使用加扰器来保护广播信号。

examples.en: Some radios need a scrambler to keep conversations private.
examples.zh: 有些无线电需要加扰器来保持对话私密。

definition.en: someone who moves quickly, climbs, or struggles in a hurried, disorganized way
definition.zh: （匆忙地）攀爬者；奔跑/挣扎者

examples.en: The scramblers reached the ridge before the weather changed.
examples.zh: 攀爬者在天气变化前到达了山脊。

examples.en: In the crowd, a few scramblers pushed forward to get a better view.
examples.zh: 在人群中，有些人急着往前挤想看得更清楚。

definition.en: a kitchen tool used to stir and break up eggs while cooking
definition.zh: （做炒蛋用的）搅蛋器；炒蛋搅拌器

examples.en: She grabbed an egg scrambler and started stirring the pan.
examples.zh: 她拿起搅蛋器，开始在锅里搅拌。

examples.en: An egg scrambler helps keep the eggs soft and fluffy.
examples.zh: 搅蛋器能让炒蛋更柔软蓬松。

extraExplanation: 在通信领域，scrambler 常指把语音/视频/数据“打乱”以防被直接读取的装置或算法（广义上接近“加密/编码”的效果，但技术实现可能不同）。
etymology: 源自动词 scramble（“乱攀爬；匆忙移动；把……搅乱/打乱”）。名词 scrambler 表示“进行 scramble 的人或装置”，复数为 scramblers。
mnemonic: 联想 scramble=“搅乱/乱爬/乱跑”；scrambler 就是“让东西变乱的工具/人”。
把 -er 当作“做这件事的人或工具”：teach→teacher；scramble→scrambler。

summary.en: moves or competes in a rushed, messy way; or makes something mixed up
summary.zh: 慌乱地赶、爬或争抢；或把东西搅乱、打乱

definition.en: moves quickly and awkwardly, often using hands and feet; or tries to get somewhere in a hurry
definition.zh: （常用手脚）快速而费力地攀爬；或匆忙赶路/挤着前进

examples.en: He scrambles up the rocks to get a better view.
examples.zh: 他手脚并用地爬上岩石，想看得更清楚。

examples.en: Commuters scramble for the last train home.
examples.zh: 通勤的人们争先恐后地赶最后一班回家的车。

definition.en: mixes things into a confused or random order
definition.zh: 把……搅乱；把……打乱顺序

examples.en: The update scrambles the file names, so it’s hard to find anything.
examples.zh: 这次更新把文件名顺序打乱了，很难找到东西。

examples.en: Too many tabs scramble my focus.
examples.zh: 标签页开太多会打乱我的注意力。

definition.en: (plural noun) disorderly rushes or struggles to get something; instances of hurried climbing or movement
definition.zh: （复数名词）争抢/混乱的抢夺；（多次）匆忙攀爬或奔忙的情况

examples.en: There were scrambles for tickets as soon as the sale opened.
examples.zh: 一开售大家就开始抢票。

examples.en: The hike includes a few short scrambles near the summit.
examples.zh: 接近山顶时有几段短距离的手脚并用攀爬。

extraExplanation: 在户外语境里，scramble 常指介于“走路”和“攀岩”之间的路线：需要用手保持平衡，但通常不需要绳索。
在烹饪里，scramble 常见于 scrambled eggs（炒鸡蛋），动词含义是“把蛋液搅散并加热炒熟”。
etymology: 源自“scramble”的变体用法，可能与古英语/中古英语中表示“抓、爬、匆忙移动”的词形有关；后来也引申出“把东西弄乱、打乱”的意思。
mnemonic: 联想中文“乱成一团/乱抓一通”：scramble = 乱 + 匆忙。
把 scram（溜走、快走）+ ble（小动、搅动）联想成“急急忙忙地动来动去，所以变乱”。

summary.en: “Scrambling” usually means hurried movement (often climbing with hands and feet), a rush/struggle to get something scarce, or cooking eggs by stirring them while they set.
summary.zh: “scrambling”常指慌忙地行动（也可指手脚并用攀爬）、对稀缺资源的争抢，或指把鸡蛋搅散炒成炒蛋。

definition.en: moving or acting quickly in a slightly confused or hurried way, often using hands and feet (for example, climbing over rocks)
definition.zh: （常指手脚并用地）匆忙地攀爬、穿越；也可指慌忙地行动

examples.en: We went scrambling up the hillside to get a better view.
examples.zh: 我们手脚并用地爬上山坡，想看得更清楚。

examples.en: She made a scrambling run for the last train.
examples.zh: 她慌忙赶去搭最后一班火车。

definition.en: the act of competing or struggling to get something scarce (money, seats, information, etc.)
definition.zh: （对稀缺资源的）争抢、抢夺

examples.en: There was a scrambling for tickets as soon as the website opened.
examples.zh: 网站一开放，大家就开始抢票。

examples.en: In a scrambling job market, networking matters a lot.
examples.zh: 在竞争激烈的就业市场里，人脉很重要。

definition.en: mixing eggs (or a similar food) while cooking to form soft curds
definition.zh: （烹饪）把鸡蛋等搅散炒成蛋花/炒蛋

examples.en: He’s scrambling eggs for breakfast.
examples.zh: 他在做早餐炒鸡蛋。

examples.en: Scrambling the eggs too long makes them dry.
examples.zh: 鸡蛋炒太久会变干。

extraExplanation: “Scrambling”来自动词“scramble”，核心感觉是“动作快、姿势不太优雅、带点慌乱”。用于户外时常指在岩石/山坡上“手脚并用地爬”，比普通的climb更强调急促和不稳定。
在英国英语里，“scrambling”也常指一种介于徒步(hiking)与攀岩(rock climbing)之间的活动：路线更陡、更需要用手辅助，但通常不需要专业攀岩装备。

summary.en: A scramjet is a high-speed jet engine that burns fuel in supersonic airflow, used for hypersonic flight research and applications.
summary.zh: scramjet 是一种在超音速气流中燃烧燃料的高速喷气发动机，常用于高超音速飞行的研究与应用。

definition.en: a type of jet engine (supersonic combustion ramjet) designed to work at very high speeds, where air flows through the engine at supersonic speed and fuel burns in that airflow
definition.zh: 一种喷气发动机（超音速燃烧冲压发动机），用于极高速度飞行；空气在发动机内以超音速流动，并在这种气流中燃烧燃料

examples.en: Scramjets are studied for hypersonic aircraft and missiles.
examples.zh: 超音速燃烧冲压发动机常用于研究高超音速飞机和导弹。

examples.en: Unlike a regular jet engine, a scramjet has no rotating compressor blades.
examples.zh: 与普通喷气发动机不同，scramjet 没有旋转的压气机叶片。

examples.en: A scramjet typically needs another system to accelerate it to high speed before it can operate well.
examples.zh: scramjet 通常需要其他系统先把速度加到很高，才能较好地工作。

extraExplanation: “scramjet”是“supersonic combustion ramjet”的缩写：supersonic（超音速）+ combustion（燃烧）+ ramjet（冲压发动机）。
学习时可用对比记忆：ramjet（冲压发动机）通常在发动机内把气流减速到亚音速再燃烧；scramjet 则让燃烧在超音速气流中进行，因此更适合更高马赫数的飞行，但技术难度也更大。
常见于航天航空与国防语境，日常英语里较少见，多用于科普文章、新闻报道或工程讨论。

summary.en: “Scrap” most commonly means a small leftover piece (a scrap of paper/food), or to discard/cancel something (scrap a plan); informally it can also mean to fight.
summary.zh: “scrap”最常见意思是“碎片/残余物”（如一张纸片、一些剩饭），也可作动词表示“废弃/取消”（如取消计划）；非正式用法还可表示“打架/争吵”。

definition.en: a small piece or leftover part of something, especially food, paper, or metal
definition.zh: 碎片；残余物（尤指食物、纸张或金属的小块）

examples.en: He fed the dog a few scraps from the table.
examples.zh: 他把餐桌上剩下的一些碎食喂给狗。

examples.en: I wrote the number on a scrap of paper.
examples.zh: 我把号码写在一张纸片上。

definition.en: to throw away, cancel, or stop using something because it is no longer needed or useful
definition.zh: 废弃；取消；停止使用（不再需要或无用的东西）

examples.en: They scrapped the old plan and started over.
examples.zh: 他们取消了旧计划，重新开始。

examples.en: The company scrapped several outdated rules.
examples.zh: 公司废除了几条过时的规定。

definition.en: to have a short, rough fight or argument (often informal)
definition.zh: 打架；争吵（多为非正式、短暂的冲突）

examples.en: Two boys were scrapping outside the store.
examples.zh: 两个男孩在商店外打了起来。

examples.en: The siblings started scrapping over the remote control.
examples.zh: 兄弟姐妹为遥控器吵了起来。

extraExplanation: 名词 scrap 常见于复数 scraps，指“零碎剩下的东西/残羹剩饭”。在环保语境里，scrap metal 指“废金属”。
动词 scrap 在英式英语中也很常用，表示“把（车辆、机器）报废拆解”，如 scrap a car（把车报废）。
scrappy（adj.）常形容“爱打架的、好斗的”，也可指“虽小但很有韧劲的”。

summary.en: discarded iron or steel collected for recycling or reuse
summary.zh: 用于回收或再利用的废旧钢铁

definition.en: discarded iron or steel (often from old machines, cars, or construction) collected to be reused or recycled
definition.zh: 废铁；废旧钢铁（常来自旧机器、汽车或建筑材料，收集后再利用或回收）

examples.en: They sold the old fence as scrap iron.
examples.zh: 他们把旧栅栏当废铁卖了。

examples.en: The scrapyard is full of scrap iron and metal parts.
examples.zh: 废品场里堆满了废铁和金属零件。

examples.en: Rising scrap iron prices made recycling more profitable.
examples.zh: 废铁价格上涨让回收更赚钱了。

extraExplanation: 常见搭配：scrap metal（废金属，范围更广）、scrap yard/scrapyard（废品场/废车场）、to sell for scrap（当废料卖）。
注意区分：scrap iron 是“废铁（材料）”，不是“iron scrap（铁的碎片）”这种随意组合；在英语里更固定用 scrap iron。
etymology: scrap 原义为“碎片、残料”，来自中古英语，表示“切下来的边角料”；后来引申为“废料”。scrap iron 指“作为废料处理的铁/钢”，常见于回收与工业语境。
mnemonic: 把 scrap 记成“碎渣/边角料”（类似中文“碎、渣”），scrap iron 就是“铁的碎渣”→废铁。
联想回收站：scrap + yard（废品场）里最常见的就是 scrap iron。

summary.en: extra or used paper for quick notes or drafts
summary.zh: 用于随手记录或打草稿的废纸/草稿纸

definition.en: paper that is not important (often used or extra) and is used for quick notes, drafts, or practice.
definition.zh: （常指用过的或多余的）废纸；用于随手记、打草稿或练习的纸。

examples.en: I wrote the phone number on a piece of scrap paper.
examples.zh: 我把电话号码写在一张废纸上。

examples.en: Use scrap paper to do the math before you write the final answer.
examples.zh: 先用废纸算一算，再写最终答案。

examples.en: She practiced the spelling words on scrap paper.
examples.zh: 她在废纸上练习拼写单词。

etymology: “scrap”原意是“碎片、残余”，来自古英语/古诺斯语相关词根，后来引申为“零碎的、不要的东西”。与“paper”组合后，就指“零碎/剩下的纸”。
mnemonic: 把 scrap 理解成“碎渣/边角料”，scrap paper 就是“纸的边角料”→ 废纸、草稿纸。
联想“scrap = scraps（剩饭剩菜）”，scrap paper 就是“剩下的纸”。

summary.en: the money something is worth as scrap after it’s no longer usable
summary.zh: 物品报废后按废料出售的残值

definition.en: the amount of money something can be sold for when it is no longer usable and is sold as scrap material (metal, parts, etc.)
definition.zh: （物品报废后按废料/废品出售的）残值；废品价值

examples.en: After the accident, the car was only worth its scrap value.
examples.zh: 事故之后，这辆车只剩下废品价值了。

examples.en: The company sold the old machines for scrap value.
examples.zh: 公司把旧机器按残值当废品卖掉了。

examples.en: Sometimes the scrap value of copper cables is surprisingly high.
examples.zh: 有时铜电缆的废品价值会高得出乎意料。

definition.en: (figurative) the small remaining value left when the main value is gone
definition.zh: （比喻）主要价值消失后所剩无几的价值

examples.en: Once the scandal broke, the brand’s reputation had only scrap value.
examples.zh: 丑闻爆发后，这个品牌的口碑几乎只剩一点“残值”。

examples.en: Without the star player, the team’s marketing appeal dropped to scrap value.
examples.zh: 没有明星球员后，球队的营销吸引力几乎跌到最低。

extraExplanation: 常见于财务/保险/二手交易语境：例如车辆“total loss（全损）”后，保险理赔可能会参考车辆的 scrap value（残值）。
与 salvage value（残值/回收价值）接近，但 salvage value 更偏“可回收/可修复后再利用”的价值范围更广；scrap value 更强调“当废料卖”。
etymology: 由 scrap（碎片、废料；也可指“报废品”）+ value（价值）组成，常用于车辆、机器等“报废后按废料出售”的估价语境。
mnemonic: 把 scrap 记成“碎渣/废料（像中文的‘废’）”，scrap value 就是“当废品卖还能值多少钱”。
联想：scrap（报废拆成零碎）+ value（价值）= “拆成废料后的价格”。

summary.en: someone who lives on or eats leftovers; sometimes used as a rude insult
summary.zh: 吃剩饭残渣的人/动物；有时作粗鲁的侮辱称呼

definition.en: A person or animal that eats leftover bits of food (scraps).
definition.zh: 吃剩饭残渣（饭菜碎屑）的人或动物。

examples.en: As a kid, he joked that he was a scrap-eater because he finished everyone’s leftovers.
examples.zh: 小时候他开玩笑说自己是“scrap-eater”，因为他会把大家的剩菜都吃完。

examples.en: The stray dog survived as a scrap-eater behind the restaurant.
examples.zh: 那只流浪狗靠在餐馆后面捡剩饭残渣活了下来。

definition.en: A harsh, informal insult for someone seen as extremely poor or living off others’ leftovers.
definition.zh: （非正式、带侮辱性）指被认为非常贫穷、靠别人剩余东西过活的人。

examples.en: The bully called him a scrap-eater, which was cruel and unfair.
examples.zh: 那个霸凌者叫他“scrap-eater”，既残忍又不公平。

examples.en: Try not to use words like scrap-eater—talk about people with respect.
examples.zh: 尽量别用“scrap-eater”这种词——谈论别人要有尊重。

extraExplanation: scrap 最常见是“（食物）残羹剩饭、碎屑”，也可指“废料/废金属”（scrap metal）。scrap-eater 通常与“吃剩饭”相关，而不是“吃废铁”。
第二个义项带强烈贬义，可能涉及阶层与贫困刻板印象；在现实交流中容易伤人，建议避免使用。
etymology: 由 scrap（碎屑、残余物、剩饭）+ eater（吃的人/动物）构成的复合词，字面意思就是“吃残渣的人/动物”。在口语里有时会被用作贬损称呼。
mnemonic: 把它拆开记：scrap（剩饭碎屑/残渣）+ eater（吃的人）=“吃残渣的人”。
联想中文“吃剩的/吃残羹剩饭”，就能抓住核心含义。

summary.en: A scrapbook is a memory-keeping book; it can also mean the hobby of making one.
summary.zh: scrapbook 指用来保存回忆的剪贴簿/纪念册；也可作动词，表示制作剪贴簿。

definition.en: A book or album where you collect and arrange items such as photos, ticket stubs, letters, or small souvenirs, often with notes, to preserve memories.
definition.zh: 剪贴簿；纪念册（把照片、票根、信件、小纪念品等收集并整理起来，常配文字，用来保存回忆）

examples.en: She made a scrapbook of her trip, with photos and train tickets.
examples.zh: 她做了一本旅行剪贴簿，里面有照片和火车票。

examples.en: My grandmother keeps a family scrapbook with old pictures and stories.
examples.zh: 我奶奶保存着一本家庭剪贴簿，里面有老照片和故事。

definition.en: To collect and arrange memories or materials in a scrapbook.
definition.zh: 把资料/回忆做成剪贴簿；剪贴成册

examples.en: They scrapbook on weekends to relax.
examples.zh: 他们周末做剪贴簿来放松。

examples.en: I’m scrapbooking our baby’s first year.
examples.zh: 我正在把宝宝的第一年做成剪贴簿。

extraExplanation: “scrapbook”由 scrap（零碎东西、剪下来的小片）+ book（书）组成。常见于手工爱好（crafts）语境，尤其是用贴纸、花边纸、标题字母等装饰的“创意剪贴簿（scrapbooking）”。
在英语文化里，scrapbook 不仅用于个人纪念，也常用于学校活动、社团、体育队等记录（例如 team scrapbook）。

summary.en: made a scrapbook; collected and arranged keepsakes or clippings
summary.zh: 做过剪贴簿；把纪念物或剪报等收集整理成册

definition.en: to make or decorate a scrapbook by arranging photos, tickets, notes, and other keepsakes (usually in the past)
definition.zh: （过去式）做剪贴簿；把照片、票据、纪念物等整理并装饰成剪贴簿

examples.en: Last winter, I scrapbooked our trip photos into a small album.
examples.zh: 去年冬天，我把我们旅行的照片做成了一个小剪贴簿。

examples.en: She scrapbooked old letters and postcards to remember her grandparents.
examples.zh: 她把旧信件和明信片做成剪贴簿，用来纪念祖父母。

definition.en: to keep a record of something by collecting and arranging related items (past)
definition.zh: （过去式）用收集、整理的方式记录某事

examples.en: He scrapbooked newspaper clippings about his hometown’s history.
examples.zh: 他把关于家乡历史的报纸剪报整理成册。

examples.en: We scrapbooked funny quotes from the class into one notebook.
examples.zh: 我们把课堂上的搞笑语录收集整理到一本笔记本里。

extraExplanation: scrapbook 通常指手工把照片、票据、贴纸、手写说明等组合在一起的纪念册；作动词时常见于谈论“做手账/相册/纪念册”的活动。
scrapbooked 是过去式/过去分词：I scrapbooked… / I have scrapbooked…；也可作形容词用法较少见（如 scrapbooked pages“做过剪贴的页面”）。
etymology: 由 scrapbook（剪贴簿）+ -ed 构成。scrap 指“零碎剪片/碎片”，book 指“书/本子”；原意就是把“零碎材料”贴进本子里保存。
mnemonic: 把 scrap（碎片/剪贴）+ book（本子）联想成“把碎片贴进本子里”。看到 -ed 就想到“已经做过了”。
联想中文“剪贴簿/手账”，scrap≈“剪”，book≈“簿”。

summary.en: “Scrape” usually means to rub or pull something off a surface, to make a harsh rubbing sound, or to barely manage to get by; as a noun it can mean a small skin abrasion.
summary.zh: “scrape”常指“刮掉/擦掉”、（摩擦）发出刺耳声，也可指“勉强凑够/勉强维持”；作名词可指“擦伤”。

definition.en: to remove something from a surface by rubbing or with a tool, often roughly or noisily
definition.zh: （用力摩擦或用工具）刮掉、擦掉（表面的东西），常带有粗糙或刺耳的感觉

examples.en: He scraped the mud off his shoes before coming inside.
examples.zh: 他进屋前把鞋上的泥刮掉了。

examples.en: I scraped the ice off the windshield with a card.
examples.zh: 我用卡片把挡风玻璃上的冰刮掉。

definition.en: to make a harsh sound as something rubs against a surface
definition.zh: （因摩擦）发出刺耳的刮擦声

examples.en: The chair scraped loudly across the floor.
examples.zh: 椅子在地板上拖动，发出很响的刮擦声。

definition.en: to barely manage to get something (money, a pass, success), usually with difficulty
definition.zh: （勉强）凑到/弄到（钱、及格、成功等），通常很费劲

examples.en: We scraped together enough money for the rent.
examples.zh: 我们东拼西凑才凑够房租。

examples.en: She scraped by on a low salary.
examples.zh: 她靠着微薄的工资勉强度日。

definition.en: a mark or injury where the skin is rubbed off; a minor abrasion
definition.zh: （皮肤）擦伤、轻微刮伤

examples.en: He got a scrape on his knee when he fell.
examples.zh: 他摔倒时膝盖擦伤了。

extraExplanation: “scrape”常和介词/副词搭配：scrape off（刮掉）、scrape together（东拼西凑）、scrape by（勉强过活/勉强通过）。其中“scrape by/together”更偏口语，表达“刚刚够”。
读音与“scrap（废料/碎片）”很像，但意思不同；“scrape”强调“刮擦动作或勉强凑够”，而“scrap”常指“碎片/废弃物”或“打架/争吵（动词）”。

summary.en: earn barely enough to survive
summary.zh: 勉强糊口；艰难维持生计

definition.en: to earn just enough money to live, usually with difficulty and not much comfort
definition.zh: （艰难地）勉强维持生计；辛苦赚到够生活的钱

examples.en: After the factory closed, he had to scrape a living doing odd jobs.
examples.zh: 工厂倒闭后，他只能靠打零工勉强维持生计。

examples.en: Many small farmers scrape a living when prices are low.
examples.zh: 当价格低迷时，许多小农户只能艰难维持生计。

examples.en: She scraped a living in the city while studying at night.
examples.zh: 她在城里一边工作一边夜校学习，勉强维持生活。

extraExplanation: 语气通常带有“钱不够用、生活不宽裕、靠零工或不稳定收入撑着”的含义。
常见搭配：scrape a living as a/an + 职业（as a taxi driver）；scrape a living doing + 事情（doing deliveries）。
etymology: 来自 scrape（“刮、擦；费力凑出”）的引申义：像把零碎的东西“刮拢”一样，艰难地凑出足够维持生活的钱；living 指“生计/生活来源”。
mnemonic: 把钱想成散落的碎屑：要“scrape（刮）”起来才能够 living（生活）。
联想中文“刮来刮去才够糊口”，对应“scrape a living”。

summary.en: barely manage with limited resources
summary.zh: 在资源不足时勉强维持

definition.en: to manage to live or succeed with only a small amount of money, time, or resources; to barely get by
definition.zh: （钱/时间/资源很少时）勉强维持；将就过；勉强应付

examples.en: After the rent went up, they could only scrape by on one income.
examples.zh: 房租涨了以后，他们只能靠一份收入勉强维持生活。

examples.en: I was so busy this week that I just scraped by on four hours of sleep a night.
examples.zh: 这周我太忙了，每晚只睡四个小时，勉强撑过去。

examples.en: He scraped by in the exam with a passing grade.
examples.zh: 他考试勉强及格。

extraExplanation: 常见搭配：scrape by on + 钱/资源（靠……勉强过日子），scrape by with + 最低限度的条件（凭……勉强应付）。
语气通常带“困难、紧巴巴”的感觉；比 get by 更强调“险些不够、很勉强”。
etymology: 来自动词 scrape（“刮、擦”），引申出“拼拼凑凑、艰难凑出一点点”的感觉；scrape by 就是“靠一点点勉强过关/过日子”。
mnemonic: 想象“刮（scrape）锅底”把最后一点饭刮出来吃＝日子过得很紧，只能勉强维持。
by 有“勉强过关/擦边过去”的感觉：scrape（刮出一点）+ by（擦边过）＝勉强过。

summary.en: remove by rubbing/dragging; or barely obtain a small amount
summary.zh: 通过刮擦把表面东西去掉；或勉强凑出一点（尤指钱）

definition.en: to remove something from a surface by rubbing or pulling, often with effort
definition.zh: （用力摩擦或刮）把……从表面去掉；刮掉

examples.en: Scrape off the mud from your shoes before you come inside.
examples.zh: 进屋前把鞋上的泥刮掉。

examples.en: He used a knife to scrape off the old paint.
examples.zh: 他用刀把旧漆刮掉。

examples.en: Try to scrape off the sticker without tearing it.
examples.zh: 尽量把贴纸刮下来，别撕破。

definition.en: to manage to get a small amount of something (especially money), often with difficulty
definition.zh: （尤指钱）勉强凑出一点；好不容易弄到一点

examples.en: We scraped off enough money for a cheap hotel.
examples.zh: 我们勉强凑够了住便宜旅馆的钱。

examples.en: She scraped off a few dollars for lunch.
examples.zh: 她好不容易凑出几美元买午饭。

extraExplanation: 常见搭配：scrape off dirt/mud/paint/sticker（刮掉污渍/泥/油漆/贴纸）。
与 scrape up（凑齐）相近：scrape up enough money（勉强凑够钱）。scrape off 更强调“从现有的一点点里抠出来/省出来”的感觉，在口语中也会出现。
etymology: “scrape”源自中古英语，和日耳曼语系中表示“刮擦”的词有关；“off”表示“离开表面/脱离”，合起来就是“把东西刮离表面”。后来也引申出“勉强凑出（资源）”的意思。
mnemonic: 把 off 理解成“离开表面”，scrape=“刮”，合起来就是“刮到离开表面”。
联想中文“刮下来/刮掉”，画面感强：刀/指甲把东西从表面“刮走”。

summary.en: remove from inside by scraping; obtain with difficulty
summary.zh: 从里面刮出；艰难地凑到/获得

definition.en: to remove something from inside a surface or container by scraping (rubbing hard with a sharp edge)
definition.zh: 把……从里面刮出来；刮除（容器/表面内侧的东西）

examples.en: She scraped the burnt rice out of the pot.
examples.zh: 她把锅里糊掉的米饭刮了出来。

examples.en: Use a spoon to scrape the seeds out of the cucumber.
examples.zh: 用勺子把黄瓜里的籽刮出来。

examples.en: He scraped the mud out of his shoes before coming in.
examples.zh: 他进门前把鞋里的泥刮了出来。

definition.en: to obtain or produce something with difficulty, often only just enough
definition.zh: 艰难地凑出/获得（某物），往往只是勉强够用

examples.en: They scraped out enough money to pay the rent.
examples.zh: 他们勉强凑够了付房租的钱。

examples.en: He scraped out a small profit in his first year.
examples.zh: 他第一年勉强赚了一点点利润。

extraExplanation: 常见结构：scrape sth out of + 容器/表面（scrape the ice cream out of the tub）。
易混短语：scrape off（把表面上的东西刮掉） vs. scrape out（把里面的东西刮出来）。
语气上常带“费劲、勉强”的感觉，尤其是第二个意思。
etymology: 由动词“scrape（刮、擦）”加副词“out（向外、从里面出来）”组成，字面意思就是“刮到外面来”，引申为“费力地弄到一点”。
mnemonic: 把“scrape”想成中文“刮”，“out”是“出来”——合起来就是“刮出来”。
第二义联想“刮一点出来”→ 只能勉强“凑出一点钱/利润”。

summary.en: manage to gather a small amount with difficulty
summary.zh: 费劲地凑出一点（钱/时间/资源等）

definition.en: to gather or collect something with difficulty, from whatever is available; to manage to produce a small amount (often money, time, or resources)
definition.zh: （费劲地）东拼西凑、勉强凑出（钱/时间/资源等）；将零散东西凑到一起

examples.en: We scraped together enough money to pay the rent.
examples.zh: 我们东拼西凑，才凑够了房租。

examples.en: She scraped together a quick meal from leftovers.
examples.zh: 她用剩菜剩饭凑合着弄了一顿饭。

examples.en: Can you scrape together some time this week to meet?
examples.zh: 你这周能不能挤出点时间见个面？

extraExplanation: 常见搭配：scrape together money/funds/cash（凑钱），scrape together a meal（凑合一顿饭），scrape together enough time（挤出时间）。
语气提示：通常暗示“数量不多 + 来得不容易”。如果只是中性地“收集”，更常用 collect / gather。
etymology: “scrape”本义是“刮、擦、用力抠出”，引申为“从很少的东西里硬挤出一点”。“scrape together”字面就是“刮着把东西凑到一起”，所以常带有“勉强、困难”的语气。
mnemonic: 联想“刮（scrape）锅底”把剩下的一点点都刮出来→就像“东拼西凑”。
“scrape”≈“刮”，把零散的东西“刮到一起”＝凑出来。

summary.en: collect by scraping or manage to obtain with difficulty
summary.zh: 刮起收拢；（勉强）凑到/筹到

definition.en: to pick up or collect something from a surface, often by scraping or scooping (especially small pieces, dirt, or leftovers)
definition.zh: （常用刮、铲、勺等）把某物从表面刮起/铲起并收拢（尤指碎屑、污垢或剩余物）

examples.en: He scraped up the spilled rice with a spoon.
examples.zh: 他用勺子把撒出来的米饭刮起来收拢。

examples.en: Could you scrape up the mud near the door before it dries?
examples.zh: 门口那块泥你能趁没干先刮起来吗？

examples.en: She scraped up the last bit of sauce with a piece of bread.
examples.zh: 她用一块面包把最后一点酱汁刮起来吃掉。

definition.en: to manage to get something with difficulty, especially a small amount of money or resources
definition.zh: （勉强）凑出/筹到（尤指少量的钱或资源）

examples.en: I scraped up enough money for a bus ticket.
examples.zh: 我勉强凑够了公交车票钱。

examples.en: We scraped up a small team to finish the project on time.
examples.zh: 我们勉强凑了个小团队按时把项目做完。

extraExplanation: 常见搭配：scrape up money / enough cash / the last bit (of food) / crumbs / dirt / mud。
语气上常带“费劲、勉强”的感觉：scrape up enough…（好不容易才够）。
etymology: “scrape”原意是“刮、擦”，加上副词“up”有“往上收拢、聚拢”的感觉，因此“scrape up”既可指把碎东西刮拢起来，也引申为“艰难地凑出（钱/人手等）”。
mnemonic: 把它想成“刮（scrape）到一起并收起来（up）”：碎屑刮拢=scrape up。
联想中文“刮一刮凑一凑”：既能刮起残渣，也能凑出一点钱。

word: scrape the bottom of the barrel

summary.en: to be forced to use the worst remaining options
summary.zh: 被迫将就使用最差的剩余选择

definition.en: To use the least desirable people, things, or resources because better options are not available; to make do with what is left.
definition.zh: 在没有更好选择时，只能用最差/最不理想的人、物或资源；将就用剩下的。

examples.en: After the best applicants accepted other offers, the company was scraping the bottom of the barrel.
examples.zh: 最优秀的应聘者都去了别的公司后，这家公司只能“将就用最差的选择”。

examples.en: By the end of the month, I was scraping the bottom of the barrel and eating instant noodles every day.
examples.zh: 到月底我都快“穷到只剩下最差的选择”了，只能天天吃方便面。

examples.en: The sequel feels like it’s scraping the bottom of the barrel for ideas.
examples.zh: 这部续集感觉是在“把点子用到山穷水尽”，只能从最差的想法里硬凑。

extraExplanation: 语气通常偏负面，常用来批评“质量下降、选择很差、已经没招了”。也可用于自嘲（比如钱快花光、食物只剩很差的选择）。
常见变体：scrape the bottom of the barrel for (something)（为了某物而不得不将就/硬凑）。
etymology: 源自字面动作：把桶（barrel）里剩下的最后一点东西用刮（scrape）的方式刮出来。引申为“好东西用完了，只剩最差的残余可用”。
mnemonic: 想象一个木桶里好东西都被拿走了，只能“刮桶底”把最后一点残渣刮出来＝只剩下最差的选择。
barrel=“桶”，bottom=“底”，scrape=“刮”；把三个词的画面连起来就很容易记。

summary.en: barely succeed or pass
summary.zh: 勉强成功或通过

definition.en: To succeed, pass, or survive with only a small margin; to barely manage something.
definition.zh: 勉强成功/勉强通过；险些撑过去。

examples.en: She scraped through the final exam with a 61.
examples.zh: 她期末考试以61分勉强通过。

examples.en: The team scraped through to the next round after a late goal.
examples.zh: 球队靠最后时刻的进球勉强晋级下一轮。

examples.en: We’ll scrape through this month if we cut our spending.
examples.zh: 如果我们减少开支，这个月还能勉强撑过去。

extraExplanation: 常见搭配：scrape through an exam/test（勉强通过考试）；scrape through to the next round/stage（勉强晋级）；scrape through financially（经济上勉强维持）。
语气：强调“差一点就失败”，比 barely pass 更口语、更有画面感。
etymology: 由动词 scrape（“刮、擦”，也有“费力弄到/勉强获得”之意）+ through（“穿过、通过”）组合而来，形象地表达“像被刮着边一样通过”，引申为“险过、勉强过关”。
mnemonic: 把它想成“擦着边(scrape)穿过去(through)”＝“险过关”。
联想中文：像“擦线过”“压线过”，都带“差一点点”的感觉。

summary.en: barely succeed or pass; manage with difficulty
summary.zh: 勉强成功或通过；费力挤过去

definition.en: to succeed, pass, or manage something with only a small margin; to barely make it
definition.zh: 勉强成功；险胜；将将过关

examples.en: He scraped through the exam with a low passing score.
examples.zh: 他考试勉强及格。

examples.en: Our team scraped through in the final minute with a lucky goal.
examples.zh: 我们队在最后一分钟靠一个运气球险胜。

examples.en: She scraped through the interview and got the job, though she wasn’t confident.
examples.zh: 她面试勉强过关并拿到了工作，尽管她并不自信。

definition.en: to get through a tight space with difficulty, often making a scraping sound or causing slight damage
definition.zh: 勉强挤过去（常伴随摩擦声或轻微刮蹭）

examples.en: The car scraped through the narrow gate.
examples.zh: 车子勉强从狭窄的门口挤过去。

examples.en: He scraped through the crowd to reach the front.
examples.zh: 他在人群中艰难挤到前面。

extraExplanation: 常见搭配：scrape through (an exam / a test / an interview) 表示“险过”；scrape through in… 表示“在……方面/以……方式险过”，如 scraped through in the last round（在最后一轮险过）。
语气通常带一点“惊险、差点失败”的感觉，比“pass”更强调“勉强”。
etymology: “scrape”原意是“刮、擦”，引申为“费力地凑到/勉强做到”；“through”表示“穿过、完成”。合起来就有“勉强过关/勉强通过”的意思。
mnemonic: 把它想成“刮着墙(scrape)才挤过去(through)”：差一点卡住，但还是过了。
联想中文“擦边过/擦线过”：和 scrape（擦、刮）很像。

summary.en: to gather with difficulty from limited sources; to cobble together quickly
summary.zh: 勉强凑齐；匆忙拼凑

definition.en: to gather (money, resources, or items) with difficulty, often from many small or limited sources
definition.zh: （尤指从零散或有限的来源）勉强凑齐、东拼西凑（钱、资源或物品）

examples.en: We managed to scrape together enough money to pay the rent.
examples.zh: 我们勉强凑够了房租。

examples.en: She scraped together spare parts to fix the old bike.
examples.zh: 她东拼西凑了一些零件把旧自行车修好了。

definition.en: to put something together quickly and imperfectly, using whatever is available
definition.zh: （用手头现有的东西）匆忙拼凑出一个不太完善的东西

examples.en: He scraped together a simple presentation the night before the meeting.
examples.zh: 他在会议前一晚匆忙拼凑了一个简单的演示。

examples.en: They scraped together a meal from leftovers.
examples.zh: 他们用剩菜凑合做了一顿饭。

extraExplanation: 语气常带有“数量不多、来得不容易”的含义，常见搭配：scrape together enough money/time/support。
近义表达有“scrape up”，意思很接近；“scrape together”更强调“凑到一起、拼在一起”的过程。
etymology: “scrape”本义是“刮、擦”，引申为“把一点点东西刮拢起来”；“together”表示“聚在一起”。合起来就有“辛苦地凑到一起”的感觉，常用于钱或资源不够时的“勉强凑齐”。
mnemonic: 把“scrape”想成“刮锅底/刮零钱”——一点点刮出来，最后才“together（凑在一起）”。
联想中文：东拼西凑=从各处“刮”一点，最后拼“在一起”。

summary.en: collect with difficulty from limited sources
summary.zh: （从有限来源中）费力凑齐

definition.en: to gather or collect something with difficulty, often from different places or sources
definition.zh: （费力地）东拼西凑/勉强凑齐某物（常来自不同地方或来源）

examples.en: We managed to scrape together enough money for the bus tickets.
examples.zh: 我们好不容易凑够了买公交车票的钱。

examples.en: She scraped together a few facts from old reports to finish the presentation.
examples.zh: 她从旧报告里东拼西凑了一些信息来完成展示。

examples.en: They scraped together volunteers at the last minute.
examples.zh: 他们在最后一刻勉强凑齐了志愿者。

extraExplanation: 常见搭配：scrape together money/cash/funds（凑钱）；scrape together information/facts（拼凑信息）；scrape together a team/crew/volunteers（勉强凑齐人手）。
语气通常强调“资源不足、过程不容易”，有时带一点无奈。
近义表达：scrape up（凑齐，口语更常见）；cobble together（临时拼凑出一个东西/计划/文章，强调“拼凑感”更强）。
etymology: “scrape”原意是“刮/擦”，引申为“把零碎的东西刮到一起”，因此“scrape together”带有“艰难凑齐、勉强拼出”的感觉。
mnemonic: 把“scrape”想成“刮刮乐/刮一刮”：把零碎的钱或东西“刮”到一起→勉强凑齐。
联想中文“东拼西凑”：together=凑在一起，但前面加scrape=凑得很辛苦。

summary.en: to gather or obtain with difficulty; or to scrape bits together
summary.zh: 勉强凑到；或把零碎东西刮拢起来

definition.en: to manage to collect or obtain (something), especially money or resources, with difficulty
definition.zh: （尤指钱或资源）勉强凑齐、好不容易筹到

examples.en: We scraped up enough money to pay the rent.
examples.zh: 我们好不容易凑够钱付房租。

examples.en: She scraped up the courage to apologize.
examples.zh: 她鼓起勇气去道歉。

examples.en: Can you scrape up a few volunteers for Saturday?
examples.zh: 你周六能不能勉强凑到几个志愿者？

definition.en: to gather (small bits of something) by scraping them together, often from a surface
definition.zh: 把零碎的东西刮拢、刮到一起（常从表面刮起）

examples.en: He scraped up the last bits of frosting from the bowl.
examples.zh: 他把碗里最后一点糖霜刮了起来。

examples.en: Scrape up the spilled rice and throw it away.
examples.zh: 把洒出来的米刮拢起来扔掉。

definition.en: to injure (a body part) by rubbing it against a rough surface
definition.zh: （在粗糙表面摩擦）擦伤、刮伤

examples.en: The kids fell and scraped up their knees.
examples.zh: 孩子们摔倒把膝盖擦伤了。

examples.en: I scraped up my elbow on the sidewalk.
examples.zh: 我在人行道上把胳膊肘擦破了。

extraExplanation: “scrape up”口语里很常见，语气常带“费劲、勉强”的感觉：scrape up enough money（勉强凑够钱）。
和“save up（攒钱）”不同：save up 强调长期、有计划地存；scrape up 强调临时、困难地凑。
etymology: “scrape”源自古诺尔斯语 skrapa，意为“刮、擦”。加上副词 up 后，常表达“把零碎的东西刮起来/凑起来”，引申为“勉强筹到（钱、人手、勇气等）”。
mnemonic: 把 up 想成“往上聚拢”：scrape（刮）+ up（拢起来）→“刮拢/凑齐”。
联想“刮彩票刮到最后一点点也要刮起来”→ 费劲地把零碎资源凑出来。

summary.en: “Scraped” usually means “rubbed/dragged roughly” or “scratched/removed by rubbing,” and it can also mean “barely collected” (scraped together) or “web-collected data” (scraped data).
summary.zh: “scraped”通常表示“刮擦/拖擦、擦伤或刮下”，也可表示“勉强凑齐”（scraped together）或“网络抓取的数据”（scraped data）。

definition.en: Past tense and past participle of “scrape”: rubbed or pulled something across a surface (often roughly), sometimes removing a thin layer or making a harsh sound.
definition.zh: “scrape”的过去式/过去分词：在表面上摩擦、刮擦或拖拽（常带粗糙感），有时会刮掉一层或发出刺耳的声音。

examples.en: I scraped my knee when I fell off my bike.
examples.zh: 我从自行车上摔下来时擦破了膝盖。

examples.en: She scraped the burnt rice from the bottom of the pot.
examples.zh: 她把锅底烧焦的米饭刮下来。

examples.en: The chair legs scraped loudly against the floor.
examples.zh: 椅子腿在地板上拖动，发出刺耳的摩擦声。

definition.en: Collected with difficulty by scraping; obtained in a minimal or barely sufficient way (often in the pattern “scraped together”).
definition.zh: 通过“刮/凑”勉强收集来的；（常用于“scraped together”）指费力凑齐、仅仅够用。

examples.en: We scraped together enough money to pay the rent.
examples.zh: 我们东拼西凑，才凑够房租。

definition.en: (Tech) Pulled (data) from a website or source automatically, usually by a program (“web scraped”).
definition.zh: （科技）用程序从网站或来源自动抓取（数据）（常说“web scraped”）。

examples.en: The dataset was scraped from public websites.
examples.zh: 这个数据集是从公开网站抓取的。

extraExplanation: “scraped”在口语里最常见的含义是“擦伤/刮伤”（scraped my knee）或“把东西刮下来”（scraped the pan）。
“scrape together”是很常用的固定搭配，强调“很勉强地凑齐”，比“collect”更有“费力、紧巴巴”的语气。
在科技语境中，“scraped”常指“爬取/抓取”网络数据；在正式写作里也常见“scraped data / web-scraped data”。使用时注意隐私、版权和网站条款。

summary.en: A tool or program used to scrape; often for extracting website data.
summary.zh: 用于刮除或（网络上）抓取数据的工具/程序。

definition.en: A person or tool/program that collects information by scraping—especially from websites.
definition.zh: 抓取者；抓取工具/程序（尤指从网站抓取信息）。

examples.en: We built a scraper to collect product prices from several online stores.
examples.zh: 我们做了一个抓取程序，用来从几家网店收集商品价格。

examples.en: Some sites block scrapers to protect their data.
examples.zh: 有些网站会屏蔽抓取工具来保护数据。

definition.en: A tool used to scrape something off a surface.
definition.zh: 刮刀；刮除工具（用于从表面刮掉附着物）。

examples.en: Use a plastic scraper to remove the sticker without scratching the glass.
examples.zh: 用塑料刮刀把贴纸刮掉，避免刮花玻璃。

examples.en: He cleaned the paint with a scraper.
examples.zh: 他用刮刀清理了油漆。

extraExplanation: 在互联网语境里，scraper 常指 web scraper（网页爬虫/数据抓取器）。它可能通过自动访问网页、解析HTML来提取信息。不同网站对抓取的态度不同，实际使用时常需要遵守网站条款、robots.txt 以及相关法律法规。
etymology: 源自动词 scrape（刮、擦、勉强凑到），加上表示“人/物”的 -er 构成 scraper，字面意思是“用来刮的东西/刮的人”。在计算机语境中引申为“从网页等处抓取数据的程序”。
mnemonic: 把 scrape 记成“刮”，-er 表示“工具/人”：scraper = “刮的工具/刮的人”。
联想“数据刮下来”：网页上的信息被 scraper “刮走”并保存。

summary.en: A scraper is usually a tool for removing material from a surface, and in computing it can mean a program that extracts data from websites.
summary.zh: scraper 常指刮削工具；在计算机领域也指从网站提取数据的抓取程序。

definition.en: A tool or device used to scrape (remove) a layer of material from a surface.
definition.zh: 刮刀；刮削器（用来把表面的一层东西刮掉的工具或装置）。

examples.en: Use a paint scraper to remove the old paint from the door.
examples.zh: 用油漆刮刀把门上的旧漆刮掉。

examples.en: The cook used a scraper to clean the dough off the counter.
examples.zh: 厨师用刮板把台面上的面团刮干净。

definition.en: In computing, a program that automatically collects information from websites (often called a “web scraper”).
definition.zh: （计算机）抓取程序；网络爬虫/网页抓取器（自动从网站收集信息的程序）。

examples.en: They built a scraper to collect product prices from online stores.
examples.zh: 他们做了一个抓取程序，从网店收集商品价格。

examples.en: Some sites block scrapers to protect their data.
examples.zh: 有些网站会封锁抓取程序来保护数据。

definition.en: A person who scrapes something, especially as a job.
definition.zh: 刮削者；清理者（尤指以此为工作的人）。

examples.en: The window scraper worked on the high-rise building all day.
examples.zh: 擦窗/刮窗工人整天在高楼上工作。

extraExplanation: 在科技语境里，scraper 常指“web scraper（网页抓取器）”。它与“crawler（爬虫）”相近：crawler 更强调“到处爬、发现网页并建立索引”，scraper 更强调“从网页中提取特定数据”。在实际使用中两者常被混用。
在网络抓取中要注意合法合规与网站条款（terms of service），以及隐私与版权问题；很多网站会通过登录、验证码、robots.txt、限流等方式限制 scraper。

summary.en: tools or things used for scraping; sometimes a casual term for very tall buildings
summary.zh: 刮刀/刮器等刮削用具；有时口语指摩天大楼

definition.en: Tools or devices used for scraping (removing something by rubbing with a hard edge).
definition.zh: 刮刀；刮器（用硬边摩擦把东西刮掉的工具）

examples.en: Use plastic scrapers to remove the sticker without scratching the glass.
examples.zh: 用塑料刮刀把贴纸刮掉，这样不会刮花玻璃。

examples.en: The kitchen drawer has scrapers for the grill and the cutting board.
examples.zh: 厨房抽屉里有用于烤架和砧板的刮器。

definition.en: People or things that scrape; in some contexts, machines that scrape surfaces (for example, road or floor scrapers).
definition.zh: 刮擦者；刮擦物；（某些语境）刮削机（如路面/地面刮削设备）

examples.en: Industrial scrapers clean dried paint off metal sheets.
examples.zh: 工业刮削设备能把金属板上的干漆清理掉。

examples.en: The noise came from scrapers under the chair legs.
examples.zh: 声音是椅子腿下面的刮擦物发出来的。

definition.en: (Informal, US) A shortened way to refer to skyscrapers (very tall buildings).
definition.zh: （非正式，美式）“skyscrapers（摩天大楼）”的简略说法

examples.en: From the bridge you can see the scrapers downtown.
examples.zh: 从桥上能看到市中心的摩天大楼。

extraExplanation: scraper 常见于家务与清洁语境：paint scraper（油漆刮刀）、dough scraper（刮板/面团刮刀）、ice scraper（除冰刮片）。
“scrapers=skyscrapers”多见于口语或特定社群表达；正式写作更常用 skyscrapers 或 high-rises。
etymology: 来自动词 scrape（刮、擦），其词根与“用硬物刮去表面物质”的动作有关；scraper 表示“用来刮的工具/刮的人或物”，scrapers 为其复数。
mnemonic: 联想中文“刮（scrape）+ 器（-er）= 刮器（scraper）”，复数加 -s 变 scrapers。
把 scrape 想成“刮（像‘刮彩票’）”，scrapers 就是“用来刮的工具们”。

summary.en: rubs/dragging that causes scraping, or minor skin injuries; also small troubles.
summary.zh: 刮擦（发出摩擦/刮掉表面）；也指轻微擦伤；还可指小麻烦/险境。

definition.en: (Third-person singular) to rub or drag something across a rough surface, often making a harsh sound or removing a thin layer.
definition.zh: （动词第三人称单数）在粗糙表面上摩擦或拖拽（某物），常发出刺耳声音或刮掉一层薄薄的表面。

examples.en: The chair scrapes the floor when you pull it back.
examples.zh: 你把椅子往后拉时，它会刮到地板。

examples.en: He scrapes ice off the windshield every winter morning.
examples.zh: 每个冬天的早晨他都会把挡风玻璃上的冰刮掉。

definition.en: (Plural) small cuts, scratches, or minor injuries on the skin.
definition.zh: （复数名词）皮肤上的小擦伤、刮伤或轻微伤口。

examples.en: After the fall, she had a few scrapes on her knees.
examples.zh: 摔倒后，她的膝盖上有几处擦伤。

examples.en: The kids came home with scrapes and muddy shoes.
examples.zh: 孩子们回家时身上有些擦伤，鞋子也全是泥。

definition.en: (Plural, informal) difficult situations or narrow escapes from trouble.
definition.zh: （复数名词，非正式）麻烦的处境；险些出事的经历。

examples.en: He’s had a few scrapes with the law, but nothing serious.
examples.zh: 他和法律有过几次小麻烦，但都不严重。

examples.en: The company got into scrapes because of poor planning.
examples.zh: 这家公司因规划不周陷入了麻烦。

etymology: 源自中古英语 scrape，可能与古诺斯语相关，核心含义是“刮、擦、摩擦”，后来引申出“擦伤（名词）”以及“麻烦/险境（比喻）”。
mnemonic: 把 scrape 记成“刮（skreɪp）”：和中文“刮”很像的动作——刮冰、刮地板。
scrapes（擦伤）= 被“刮”出来的小伤：先有 scrape（刮擦动作），结果就是 scrapes（擦伤痕）。

summary.en: “scrapian” is not a standard English word; it’s probably a misspelling (often of “scrap iron” or “scrappy”).
summary.zh: “scrapian”不是标准英语单词，通常是拼写错误（常见想写的是“scrap iron”或“scrappy”）。

definition.en: Not a standard English word. It is likely a misspelling or a name. Common possibilities: “scrap iron” (metal waste) or “scrappy” (tough, determined).
definition.zh: 不是标准英语单词。更可能是拼写错误或专有名词。常见可能：scrap iron（废铁）或 scrappy（顽强的、好斗的）。

examples.en: I couldn’t find “scrapian” in a dictionary—did you mean “scrap iron”?
examples.zh: 我在词典里找不到“scrapian”——你是不是想说“scrap iron（废铁）”？

examples.en: If you meant “scrappy,” it can describe a small but determined team.
examples.zh: 如果你想写的是“scrappy”，它可以形容一支虽小但很有拼劲的队伍。

extraExplanation: “scrap iron”指可回收的废金属（尤其是铁），在日常生活里常见于回收站、修车厂等语境。
如果你提供一句包含“scrapian”的原句或说明你想表达的中文意思，我可以帮你确认正确拼写并给出更准确的词条。

summary.en: “Scraping” can mean the action of scraping, the scrapings you remove, or (in computing) extracting data from websites (web scraping).
summary.zh: “scraping”可指刮除的动作、刮下来的残渣（scrapings），也可指（计算机）从网站提取数据的“网页抓取/爬取”。

definition.en: the act or process of removing something from a surface by rubbing or pulling with a sharp edge; also, the material removed
definition.zh: （用刮刀/锐边）刮下、刮除的动作或过程；也可指刮下来的东西（残渣）

examples.en: There were paint scrapings on the floor after we cleaned the wall.
examples.zh: 我们清理墙面后，地上有一些刮下来的油漆碎屑。

examples.en: The archaeologist collected scrapings from the pottery to test for residue.
examples.zh: 考古学家从陶器表面刮取样本，用来检测残留物。

definition.en: present participle of “scrape”: to rub a surface hard with something sharp to remove dirt, paint, or other material; or to make a harsh sound by rubbing
definition.zh: “scrape”的现在分词：刮、擦刮以清除污垢/油漆等；也可指摩擦发出刺耳的声音

examples.en: She’s scraping the old paint off the window frame.
examples.zh: 她正在把窗框上的旧油漆刮掉。

examples.en: The chair legs were scraping against the floor.
examples.zh: 椅子腿在地板上摩擦，发出刺耳的声音。

definition.en: (computing) extracting data from websites or documents, often automatically (e.g., “web scraping”)
definition.zh: （计算机）从网站或文档中提取数据，常指自动化进行（如“网页爬取/网络抓取”）

examples.en: The team is scraping product prices from several websites for comparison.
examples.zh: 团队正在从多个网站抓取商品价格来做对比。

examples.en: Some sites block scraping to protect their content and users.
examples.zh: 有些网站会阻止抓取，以保护内容和用户。

extraExplanation: 在日常英语里，scraping既可指“正在刮”（动词现在分词），也常指“刮下来的碎屑/刮取物”（名词，常用复数scrapings）。
在科技语境中，scraping常用来指web scraping（网页数据抓取）。在真实使用中要注意合法合规与网站条款（Terms of Service），以及对隐私数据的保护。

summary.en: small bits removed by scraping; figuratively, a barely sufficient amount
summary.zh: 刮下来的碎屑；引申为勉强够用的一点点

definition.en: small bits or thin layers that are removed by scraping a surface (often seen as leftovers or waste)
definition.zh: （刮、擦表面时）刮下来的碎屑；刮落物（常指剩下的、不太值钱的部分）

examples.en: He swept the paint scrapings into the trash.
examples.zh: 他把刮下来的油漆碎屑扫进了垃圾桶。

examples.en: The recipe says to save the scrapings from the bowl and add them to the sauce.
examples.zh: 食谱说把碗壁刮下来的残料留着，加到酱汁里。

examples.en: The workshop floor was covered with metal scrapings.
examples.zh: 车间地面上满是刮下来的金属屑。

definition.en: money, food, or resources that are barely enough; a very small amount gathered with difficulty
definition.zh: 勉强凑到的一点钱/食物/资源；微薄之物

examples.en: They lived on the scrapings of a small pension.
examples.zh: 他们靠一份小额养老金的微薄收入生活。

examples.en: After rent and bills, we’re left with just scrapings.
examples.zh: 交完房租和账单后，我们就只剩一点点了。

extraExplanation: scrapings 通常是复数形式，强调“很多小碎屑/残余”。在口语或写作里，也常带有“没什么价值、只剩一点点”的语气。
相关搭配：paint scrapings（油漆刮屑）、metal scrapings（金属屑）、scrapings from the bowl（从碗里刮下来的残料）。
etymology: 来自动词 scrape（刮、擦）+ -ing（表示动作或结果），scrapings 指“刮出来的东西/刮下来的残屑”，也可引申为“所剩无几的微薄之物”。
mnemonic: 联想 scrape＝“刮”，scrapings＝“刮下来的东西”：刮（scrape）完地板/锅底，剩下的就是碎屑（scrapings）。
把 scrap（碎片）+ -ings（一堆）联想成“很多小碎片”。

summary.en: “Scrapped” usually means “thrown away or cancelled,” and it can also (informally) mean “fought.”
summary.zh: “scrapped”通常表示“报废/丢弃”或“取消计划”，也可（非正式）表示“打架/争吵”。

definition.en: past tense/past participle of “scrap”: to throw away or get rid of something because it is no longer useful.
definition.zh: “scrap”的过去式/过去分词：把某物当废品处理；因不再有用而丢弃。

examples.en: They scrapped the old computer because it couldn’t run the new software.
examples.zh: 他们把旧电脑报废了，因为它跑不动新软件。

examples.en: Most of the broken bikes were scrapped and sold for metal.
examples.zh: 大多数坏掉的自行车都被当废品处理并按金属卖掉。

definition.en: past tense/past participle of “scrap”: to cancel, abandon, or stop a plan or project.
definition.zh: “scrap”的过去式/过去分词：取消、放弃或停止某个计划/项目。

examples.en: The company scrapped the proposal after public criticism.
examples.zh: 公司在公众批评后取消了该提案。

examples.en: We scrapped our weekend trip because of the storm.
examples.zh: 因为暴风雨，我们取消了周末旅行。

definition.en: (informal) past tense of “scrap”: to fight or argue, especially in a rough or noisy way.
definition.zh: （非正式）“scrap”的过去式：打架或激烈争吵（常指粗鲁、吵闹）。

examples.en: The two players scrapped after the final whistle.
examples.zh: 终场哨响后两名球员打了起来。

examples.en: They scrapped over who should pay the bill.
examples.zh: 他们为谁该付账而争吵。

definition.en: (adjective) thrown away, cancelled, or removed from use.
definition.zh: （形容词）被丢弃的；被取消的；被停用的。

examples.en: The scrapped cars were piled up behind the factory.
examples.zh: 报废的汽车堆在工厂后面。

examples.en: The scrapped plan was replaced with a simpler one.
examples.zh: 被取消的计划被一个更简单的计划取代了。

extraExplanation:
容易混淆：scrapped ≠ scraped。
- scrapped 来自 scrap（丢弃/报废/取消）。
- scraped 来自 scrape（刮擦、擦伤；也可指“勉强凑到/通过”，如 scraped by）。
例：I scrapped the idea（我放弃了想法） vs. I scraped my knee（我擦破了膝盖）。

相关词：
- scrap (n.) 废料；碎片；小纸条：a scrap of paper（一小片纸）
- scrap yard 废品回收场/废车场
- scrap metal 废金属

summary.en: A tough, determined fighter or someone connected with scrap and recycling.
summary.zh: 硬拼的斗士；或与废品回收相关的人。

definition.en: A person who fights in a determined, tough way; someone who doesn’t give up easily.
definition.zh: （非正式）硬拼的人；不轻易认输的斗士。

examples.en: Even though he was smaller, he was a real scrapper in the ring.
examples.zh: 虽然他个子更小，但在擂台上他真是个硬拼的斗士。

examples.en: Our team is full of scrappers who keep playing hard until the end.
examples.zh: 我们队里全是那种拼到最后一刻也不放弃的人。

definition.en: A person who collects or deals in scrap (old metal, paper, etc.) for recycling or resale.
definition.zh: 收破烂的人；做废品回收（废金属、废纸等）生意的人。

examples.en: A scrapper came by to buy old copper pipes.
examples.zh: 有个收废品的人来收购旧铜管。

examples.en: He started as a scrapper and later opened a recycling business.
examples.zh: 他从收废品做起，后来开了家回收公司。

definition.en: Someone who is quick to argue or start fights; a brawler.
definition.zh: 爱吵架或爱打架的人；斗殴者。

examples.en: He can be a scrapper when he’s had too much to drink.
examples.zh: 他喝多了就容易变得爱找事、爱打架。

extraExplanation: 拼写提示：scrapper 常见于口语，表示“硬拼的人/斗士”很常用；表示工具时更常见的是 scraper（刮刀、刮板）。
语体提示：表示“爱打架的人”时可能带贬义；表示“斗士、拼命三郎”时多为褒义。
etymology: 来自动词 scrap（打架；把……当废品处理）+ 表示“人”的后缀 -er。scrap 源自表示“碎片、残片”的含义，后来引申出“打架（像一团乱）”以及“废品、边角料”的用法。
mnemonic: 把 scrap 记成“碎片/废品/打架”，scrap + -er → “跟 scrap 有关的人”：要么收废品，要么爱打架。
联想：scrap（乱打）→ scrapper（硬拼的人），像“拼到只剩碎片也不认输”。

summary.en: tough fighters or determined people; also scrap collectors
summary.zh: 好斗或很能拼的人；也指收废品/回收废料的人

definition.en: People who fight hard or argue strongly; tough, determined people.
definition.zh: 好斗的人；拼命三郎；很有韧性、很能拼的人。

examples.en: Even when they were behind, the team played like scrappers and never quit.
examples.zh: 即使落后，这支队也像拼命三郎一样坚持不放弃。

examples.en: She’s a scrapper—she worked two jobs to pay for school.
examples.zh: 她很能拼——为了上学她打了两份工。

definition.en: People who collect, sort, or deal in scrap materials (like metal) for recycling.
definition.zh: 收废品的人；回收废料（如废金属）的人。

examples.en: Local scrappers picked up old appliances for the metal.
examples.zh: 当地收废品的人把旧家电收走回收金属。

examples.en: The city created new rules for scrappers to reduce theft and protect residents.
examples.zh: 市政府为收废品者制定了新规定，以减少盗窃并保护居民。

extraExplanation: 在美式英语里，scrapper 常用来夸人“很能拼、很有韧劲”，语气多为正面。
表示“收废品的人”时，中性描述更常见；在一些语境里也可能与“非法收购金属”相关的新闻一起出现，需要注意语境。
etymology: 源自英语 scrap（碎片、废料）。scrapper 原指“打架的人、斗士”（像在碎片中拼搏），也可指“与废料打交道的人”。复数为 scrappers。
mnemonic: 联想 scrap=“废料/碎片”；scrapper=“跟废料打交道的人”→收废品的人。
把 scrap 想成“刮擦(scrape)后留下的碎屑”，scrapper 就是“跟碎屑/硬仗打交道的人”→能拼的人。

summary.en: More tough and determined; sometimes more messy or shabby.
summary.zh: 更顽强更敢拼；有时也指更凌乱或更破旧。

definition.en: More determined and willing to fight or work hard, especially despite being smaller or having fewer resources.
definition.zh: 更“拼”、更敢冲敢干的（尤指在个头更小或资源更少时仍很顽强）。

examples.en: Their team is smaller, but scrappier, and they rarely give up.
examples.zh: 他们队伍更小，但更顽强，很少放弃。

examples.en: We need a scrappier approach if we want to compete with bigger companies.
examples.zh: 如果想和大公司竞争，我们需要更拼、更灵活的做法。

definition.en: More messy, untidy, or made of scraps; shabbier in appearance.
definition.zh: 更凌乱的、更破旧的；像由碎料拼凑的。

examples.en: After the road trip, the car looked scrappier than before.
examples.zh: 公路旅行后，车看起来比以前更破旧、更乱。

examples.en: His notes were scrappier, but the ideas were great.
examples.zh: 他的笔记更零碎凌乱，但想法很棒。

extraExplanation: scrappier 常用于体育、商业和职场语境，强调“以小博大”的韧劲和不服输的气质（类似“很能打”“很拼”）。
口语里形容公司/团队“scrappy”通常是褒义：资源少但执行力强、行动快。形容外观“scrappy”则更偏负面：邋遢、破旧。
etymology: 源自形容词 scrap（碎片、废料）→ scrappy（像碎片一样零散；引申为“爱打架、很拼”）。scrappier 是 scrappy 的比较级。
mnemonic: 联想 scrap=“碎片/废料”，scrappy=“像碎片一样零零散散”→“条件差但还能凑合干/很拼”。
把 scrappy 记成“scrap + -y（……的）”：scrappy=“碎片化的/破破的”；在人物性格上引申为“敢打敢拼”。

summary.en: Scrapping usually means (1) fighting in a rough, informal way, or (2) throwing away/ending something as useless, often by dismantling it for scrap.
summary.zh: Scrapping 常指（1）打架斗殴，或（2）把某物报废作废、丢弃不用，常伴随拆解回收。

definition.en: fighting (usually in a small, rough, informal way); taking part in a fight
definition.zh: 打架；（通常指小规模、粗暴、非正式的）斗殴

examples.en: The kids were scrapping behind the school, so a teacher broke it up.
examples.zh: 孩子们在学校后面打架，于是老师把他们拉开了。

examples.en: Two drivers started scrapping after the minor accident.
examples.zh: 两名司机在小事故后开始动手了。

definition.en: throwing something away because it is no longer useful; deciding to stop using a plan or idea
definition.zh: 把……当废品丢弃；（计划/想法）作废、取消

examples.en: They’re scrapping the old computers and buying new ones.
examples.zh: 他们要把旧电脑报废，然后买新的。

examples.en: The company is scrapping the original design after many complaints.
examples.zh: 在大量投诉后，公司决定取消原来的设计。

definition.en: taking something apart for parts or recycling (as scrap metal)
definition.zh: 拆解（机器/车辆等）用于零件回收或废金属回收

examples.en: He makes money by scrapping old cars for metal.
examples.zh: 他靠拆解旧车回收金属赚钱。

examples.en: The ship was sold to a yard for scrapping.
examples.zh: 那艘船被卖到拆船厂报废拆解。

extraExplanation: “scrapping”常被和“scraping”混淆：scrapping = 打架/报废/拆解；scraping = 刮、擦、刮出声音（如：scraping the ice off the windshield）。

summary.en: Scrappy usually means determined and ready to fight or work hard despite disadvantages; it can also mean messy or not well organized.
summary.zh: Scrappy常指在不利条件下依然很拼、很有斗志；也可指凌乱、潦草或不成体系。

definition.en: Determined and willing to fight or work hard, even when you are smaller, weaker, or have fewer resources.
definition.zh: （尤指在条件不利时）很拼、很有斗志的；不服输的。

examples.en: They’re a scrappy team that never gives up.
examples.zh: 他们是一支很拼的球队，永不放弃。

examples.en: She’s scrappy—she’ll argue for what she believes in.
examples.zh: 她很有斗志，会为自己相信的事情据理力争。

definition.en: Messy, untidy, or made of many small pieces; not neat or well organized.
definition.zh: 凌乱的；不整洁的；（由零碎部分拼成而）不成体系的。

examples.en: His handwriting is scrappy and hard to read.
examples.zh: 他的字很潦草，难以辨认。

examples.en: The essay felt scrappy, like a group of notes put together.
examples.zh: 这篇文章显得很零碎，像把一堆笔记拼在一起。

extraExplanation: “scrappy”常用来夸人或团队“以小搏大、很能拼”，在体育、职场、创业语境里很常见，带有积极色彩。
表示“凌乱/潦草”时语气更中性或略带批评，常修饰writing（字迹）、draft（草稿）、notes（笔记）等。

summary.en: Scraps are small leftover pieces, or (figuratively) small, unfair amounts given to someone.
summary.zh: Scraps 指零碎的剩余部分，也可引申为不公平地给人的一点点（施舍般的）东西。

definition.en: Small leftover pieces of something (often food, paper, cloth, etc.) that are not the main or whole part.
definition.zh: （常指食物、纸张、布料等的）碎片、边角料、剩下的小部分。

examples.en: I fed the dog some scraps from dinner.
examples.zh: 我把晚饭剩下的一些碎食喂给了狗。

examples.en: There were scraps of paper all over the floor.
examples.zh: 地板上到处都是纸屑。

examples.en: She made a quilt from fabric scraps.
examples.zh: 她用布头拼成了一床被子。

definition.en: Very small or unfair amounts given to someone, especially when they deserve more.
definition.zh: （尤指本应得到更多却只拿到的）一点点、少量（常带“不公平/可怜”的语气）。

examples.en: They threw him scraps of attention when it benefited them.
examples.zh: 他们只有在对自己有利时才给他一点点关注。

examples.en: She didn’t want scraps of praise—she wanted real feedback.
examples.zh: 她不想要零星的夸奖——她想要真正的反馈。

definition.en: To fight or argue in a rough way (often used with “scrap” as a verb in some contexts).
definition.zh: 打架；争吵（作动词时常用 scrap；scraps 可指多次小冲突的情况）。

examples.en: The two boys got into scraps after school.
examples.zh: 两个男孩放学后起了几次小冲突。

extraExplanation: “scraps”最常见是名词复数，表示“剩下的碎片/边角料”。在口语里也常用来表达“被施舍的一点点”（带贬义或不满）。
相关搭配：food scraps（厨余/剩饭菜）、scrap paper（废纸）、scraps of information（零碎信息）、left with scraps（只剩下些微不足道的东西）。
文学与常见表达中，scraps 常用来营造“零散、贫乏、被忽视”的感觉，例如“scraps of memory / scraps of conversation”（零碎记忆/片段对话）。

summary.en: A scrapyard is a place where old metal, cars, or machines are collected for sale or recycling; it can also be used figuratively for unwanted or outdated things.
summary.zh: scrapyard 指收集旧金属、报废汽车或机器并出售/回收的场地；也可作比喻，指堆放无用或过时事物的地方。

definition.en: a place where old or broken metal, cars, machines, etc. are collected and sold (often for recycling)
definition.zh: 废品场；废金属回收场（收集并出售旧的或损坏的金属、汽车、机器等，常用于回收）

examples.en: They took the damaged car to a scrapyard.
examples.zh: 他们把坏掉的车拖到废品场去了。

examples.en: The old factory has become a scrapyard full of rusting machinery.
examples.zh: 那座旧工厂变成了一个废品场，里面堆满了生锈的机器。

definition.en: (figurative) a place or situation where old, unwanted, or broken things (or ideas) end up
definition.zh: （比喻）“垃圾堆”；旧的、无用的或破败的东西（或观念）的归宿

examples.en: The report called the system a ‘scrapyard of outdated rules.’
examples.zh: 报告称这个体系是“一堆过时规定的垃圾场”。

examples.en: His desk looked like a scrapyard of broken gadgets.
examples.zh: 他的桌子看起来像一个堆满坏掉小工具的废品场。

extraExplanation: “scrapyard”由 scrap（废料、碎料）+ yard（场地）构成。英式英语里也常说 junkyard，尤其指“汽车拆解场/旧车场”。
在文化语境中，scrapyard 常让人联想到“生锈的金属、报废汽车、被拆解的机器”，也常用于比喻“过时、被淘汰”的事物或制度。

summary.en: places that collect and recycle or dismantle scrap, especially metal and old cars
summary.zh: 回收并拆解废料（尤指金属和旧车）的场地

definition.en: places where old, broken, or unwanted things (especially metal, cars, and machines) are collected to be sold, recycled, or taken apart for parts
definition.zh: 废品回收场；拆车场；堆放旧金属/旧机器等并回收利用或拆解零件的地方

examples.en: He bought a used door from a scrapyard to fix his shed.
examples.zh: 他从废品回收场买了一扇二手门来修理棚屋。

examples.en: The police searched nearby scrapyards for the stolen motorcycle.
examples.zh: 警方在附近的拆车场/废品场寻找被盗的摩托车。

examples.en: Many old cars end up in scrapyards and get recycled.
examples.zh: 很多旧车最终会进拆车场并被回收利用。

extraExplanation: “scrapyards”是复数形式；单数是“scrapyard”。
在美式英语里，“scrapyard”常与“junkyard”接近，但“scrapyard”更强调金属废料回收与拆解；“junkyard”更泛指各种旧物堆放场。
etymology: 由 scrap（碎片、废料）+ yard（场地、院子）组成；最初指堆放废料的场地，后来常特指收购旧金属、拆解旧车和机器的地方。
mnemonic: 把 scrap 记成“碎渣/废料”，yard 记成“院子/场地”，合起来就是“废料的场地”。
联想中文“废品站/回收站”：很多金属会被当 scrap 送进 yard。

summary.en: Scratch can mean a small mark/cut, the action of rubbing with nails or damaging a surface, and (in “from scratch”) starting from the beginning.
summary.zh: scratch 可指划痕/擦伤，也可指挠或刮伤；在 from scratch 中表示“从零开始/从头做起”。

definition.en: A thin mark or light cut on a surface (like skin, wood, or a screen).
definition.zh: （在皮肤、木头、屏幕等表面上的）划痕；浅浅的刮痕/擦伤。

examples.en: There’s a scratch on my phone screen.
examples.zh: 我的手机屏幕上有一道划痕。

examples.en: The cat left scratches on the sofa.
examples.zh: 猫在沙发上留下了抓痕。

definition.en: To rub with nails or a sharp object, often to stop itching.
definition.zh: （用指甲或尖物）抓；挠（常为止痒）。

examples.en: Try not to scratch the mosquito bite.
examples.zh: 尽量别去挠蚊子包。

examples.en: He scratched his arm while thinking.
examples.zh: 他一边思考一边挠了挠胳膊。

definition.en: To remove or damage a surface by scraping it.
definition.zh: 刮伤；擦伤（使表面受损）。

examples.en: Be careful not to scratch the table.
examples.zh: 小心别把桌子刮花了。

definition.en: Made without anything prepared before; starting from the very beginning (often in “from scratch”).
definition.zh: 从零开始的；完全靠自己从头做的（常用于 from scratch）。

examples.en: She made the cake from scratch.
examples.zh: 她从头开始做了这个蛋糕（不是用预拌粉）。

examples.en: They built the company from scratch.
examples.zh: 他们白手起家创办了这家公司。

extraExplanation: 常见短语：from scratch = “从零开始/从头做起”。在做饭语境里强调“不用半成品或预制材料”；在工作/学习语境里强调“没有基础，从头建立”。
名词 scratch 也可指“刮擦声”，如 the scratch of a pen（笔尖摩擦的沙沙声），属于较文学/描写性用法。

summary.en: to rummage or search casually; sometimes to scrape by by finding small opportunities
summary.zh: 随意翻找；也可指东拼西凑找机会以勉强应付

definition.en: to search for something in a place in a casual or not very organized way, often by moving things around
definition.zh: （在某处）随意地翻找、乱翻，试着找某物（常把东西拨来拨去）

examples.en: I scratched around in my bag for my keys, but they weren’t there.
examples.zh: 我在包里翻找钥匙，但不在里面。

examples.en: She scratched around in the drawer until she found the old photo.
examples.zh: 她在抽屉里乱翻，直到找到那张旧照片。

definition.en: to do small tasks or look for small opportunities without a clear plan; to get by with limited resources
definition.zh: （缺少计划地）做些零碎事、东拼西凑地找机会；在资源有限时勉强应付

examples.en: After he lost his job, he scratched around for temporary work.
examples.zh: 失业后，他四处找些临时工作来应付。

examples.en: We’re scratching around, trying to finish the project with a small budget.
examples.zh: 我们在经费紧张的情况下勉强把项目做完。

definition.en: (of animals) to scratch the ground to find food
definition.zh: （动物）刨地觅食

examples.en: The chickens scratched around in the yard for insects.
examples.zh: 鸡在院子里刨地找虫子吃。

etymology: “scratch”原意是“抓/刮”，来自中古英语；“around”表示“在周围/到处”。合在一起就有“到处抓刮、翻找”的画面，后来也引申为“东拼西凑地找办法/找机会”。
mnemonic: 把画面记住：用手“抓抓（scratch）”并且“到处（around）”翻——就是“乱翻找”。
联想“鸡刨地”：鸡用爪子scratch around地面找吃的→引申为人“到处找资源/机会”。

summary.en: search in a messy, hurried way
summary.zh: 慌乱地到处翻找

definition.en: to search for something in an unorganized or hurried way (often by moving things with your hands)
definition.zh: （常用手）乱翻、四处摸索着找某物（不太有条理或有点急）

examples.en: I scratched around for my keys in my bag, but they weren’t there.
examples.zh: 我在包里乱翻找钥匙，但没找到。

examples.en: She scratched around for a pen on the messy desk.
examples.zh: 她在乱糟糟的桌子上摸索着找一支笔。

extraExplanation: 常见结构：scratch around for + 名词（要找的东西）。
语气通常偏口语，强调“找得有点乱/急/没头绪”。
相关但不同：scratch around for ideas 表示“绞尽脑汁/东拼西凑找灵感”。
etymology: 源自 scratch（“抓、刮”）的动作意象：像用爪子/手指在周围“刮来刮去”一样去找东西，因此引申为“胡乱翻找”。
mnemonic: 联想“scratch=抓挠”，像猫在地上抓来抓去找东西 → 乱翻找。
around=到处；for=为了找 → “到处抓挠着找”。

summary.en: help someone with an expectation of a return favor; mutual favors
summary.zh: 互相帮忙（常含互惠或利益交换意味）

definition.en: to help someone, often with the expectation that they will help you in return; to do each other favors
definition.zh: 帮某人一个忙（常含“你帮我、我帮你”的互惠意味）；互相照应/互相给好处

examples.en: If you scratch my back, I’ll scratch yours—can you put in a good word for me?
examples.zh: 你帮我一把，我也帮你一把——你能替我美言几句吗？

examples.en: The two departments kept scratching each other’s backs to get their budgets approved.
examples.zh: 两个部门互相“照应”，好让各自的预算获批。

examples.en: She refused to scratch anyone’s back just to get promoted.
examples.zh: 她拒绝为了升职去讨好任何人、做利益交换。

definition.en: (literal) to rub or scrape a person’s back to relieve an itch
definition.zh: （字面义）给某人挠背/抓背（止痒）

examples.en: My back itches—could you scratch my back for a second?
examples.zh: 我背痒——你能帮我挠一下吗？

examples.en: He scratched the child’s back gently so she could fall asleep.
examples.zh: 他轻轻给孩子挠背，哄她入睡。

synonyms: you scratch my back and I’ll scratch yours
extraExplanation: 在口语与新闻中，这个短语常带一点负面或怀疑色彩，暗示“利益交换、走后门、互相包庇”。在正式场合想表达中性“互相帮助”，也可以用 help each other / do each other a favor。
etymology: 源自字面动作“挠背”。因为人通常难以自己挠到背部，需要他人帮助，于是引申为“互相帮忙、互通有无”。在英语里常见对偶结构：If you scratch my back, I’ll scratch yours。
mnemonic: 联想：背上痒自己够不到→需要别人帮你“挠”→你也会回报对方（互惠）。
把 it’s a back-scratching deal 记成“挠背式交易”＝人情/利益交换。

summary.en: be puzzled or think hard to understand something
summary.zh: 感到困惑或努力思考以弄明白

definition.en: to feel confused or puzzled; to think hard because you do not understand something
definition.zh: 感到困惑；想不明白；绞尽脑汁思考

examples.en: I’ve been scratching my head over this math problem for an hour.
examples.zh: 这道数学题我想了一个小时还是想不明白。

examples.en: Everyone was scratching their heads when the app suddenly stopped working.
examples.zh: 当这个应用突然不能用时，大家都一头雾水。

definition.en: (sometimes literal) to rub your head with your fingers, often as a sign of uncertainty or thought
definition.zh: （有时指字面动作）用手挠头，常表示不确定或在思考

examples.en: He scratched his head and asked, “Do you mean today or tomorrow?”
examples.zh: 他挠了挠头，问：“你是说今天还是明天？”

examples.en: She scratched her head, trying to remember his name.
examples.zh: 她挠挠头，努力想起他的名字。

extraExplanation: 常见搭配：scratch your head over/about something（为某事想不明白）。
语气通常中性偏口语，也可以带一点“这事真让人费解”的感觉。
etymology: 这是一个英语习语，来自人们在困惑或用力思考时常会“挠头”的真实肢体动作；后来固定为比喻用法，表示“想不通/困惑”。
mnemonic: 把它当成画面记忆：想不通的时候下意识“挠头”。
联想中文“挠头/抓耳挠腮”，意思非常接近。

summary.en: to cross out, or to manage/produce with difficulty
summary.zh: 划掉；（也指）艰难获得/勉强维持

definition.en: to remove words or marks by scratching them out (crossing them out)
definition.zh: （用划线等）把文字或痕迹划掉；删去

examples.en: He scratched out the wrong answer and wrote the correct one.
examples.zh: 他把错误答案划掉，写上了正确的。

examples.en: Please scratch out your old address on the form.
examples.zh: 请把表格上的旧地址划掉。

definition.en: to manage to get or earn something with difficulty (often a living)
definition.zh: 勉强获得；艰难维持（常指谋生）

examples.en: They scratched out a living by doing odd jobs.
examples.zh: 他们靠打零工勉强维持生计。

examples.en: The team scratched out a win in the final seconds.
examples.zh: 球队在最后几秒艰难地拿下胜利。

definition.en: to write or draw something quickly and not very neatly
definition.zh: 草草写下；潦草画出

examples.en: I scratched out a quick note before I left.
examples.zh: 我离开前匆匆写了张便条。

examples.en: She scratched out a map on a napkin.
examples.zh: 她在餐巾纸上随手画了张地图。

extraExplanation: 常见固定搭配：scratch out a living（艰难谋生）。语气上强调“条件艰苦、来之不易”。
和 cross out 很接近：cross out 更中性，表示“划掉”；scratch out 更强调用力刮划，带一点“粗糙、急促”的感觉。
etymology: 由 scratch（抓、刮、划）+ out 组成的短语动词。最早是字面意义“刮掉/划掉”，后来引申为“艰难弄到（尤指生计）”，也可指“匆忙潦草地写/画”。
mnemonic: 联想“scratch=抓挠/刮”，把字“刮出去(out)”就是“划掉”。
记搭配：scratch out a living＝“刮”出一口饭吃→勉强谋生。

summary.en: to understand or address only the basic part of something
summary.zh: 只了解或处理表面部分；不深入

definition.en: to learn or deal with only the most basic part of something, without understanding the deeper or more complex details
definition.zh: 只触及表面；略知一二；没有深入了解（或处理）

examples.en: I’ve read a few articles, but I’ve only scratched the surface of the topic.
examples.zh: 我读了几篇文章，但对这个话题也只是略知一二。

examples.en: The training covers the basics; real projects will show you how much more there is beyond the surface.
examples.zh: 培训只讲基础；真正做项目时你会发现还有很多更深的内容。

examples.en: We can’t fix the problem if we only scratch the surface and ignore the root cause.
examples.zh: 如果我们只治标不治本，就无法解决问题。

synonyms: only the tip of the iceberg
extraExplanation: 常用于学习、研究、数据分析、心理/社会问题等语境，强调“还有很多没看到/没理解”。
可与“deeper, beneath the surface, root cause”等词搭配，形成对比：scratch the surface（浅） vs. dig deeper（深）。
etymology: 源自“刮开表面”的字面动作：只轻轻划破外层，并没有挖到里面。后来引申为对知识、问题或情况了解不深。
mnemonic: 想象“刮彩票”只刮了一点点：只是看到表面，大奖信息还没刮出来＝只了解皮毛。
surface=“表面/表层”，scratch=“轻轻刮/挠”，合起来就是“只刮表面”。

summary.en: deal with only the most basic, shallow part of something
summary.zh: 只触及表面、略知皮毛

definition.en: To learn, show, or deal with only a small part of something; not go into depth.
definition.zh: 对某事只了解/涉及到表面；浅尝辄止，没有深入。

examples.en: The documentary is interesting, but it only scratches the surface of the issue.
examples.zh: 这部纪录片很有意思，但只触及了这个问题的表面。

examples.en: I’ve read one article, so I’m just scratching the surface of the topic.
examples.zh: 我只读了一篇文章，所以对这个话题还只是略知一二。

examples.en: Their apology scratches the surface of what needs to change.
examples.zh: 他们的道歉只是触及了需要改变的东西的表面。

extraExplanation: 常用结构：scratch the surface of + 名词（an issue / a problem / a subject / a culture / a mystery 等）。
语气上常暗示“实际内容远比目前看到的更复杂、更深”。
近义表达：only touch on / barely cover / just skim the surface（也常说 scratch the surface）。
etymology: 来自“scratch（抓/刮）+ surface（表面）”的字面画面：只在表面轻轻刮一下，没有深入到里面。后来引申为“只触及皮毛”。
mnemonic: 联想“刮痧/挠一下皮肤”：只是在皮肤表面轻轻一刮——= 只了解皮毛。
surface=表面；scratch=抓。把画面记住：只抓到表面。

summary.en: searching for food by scratching the ground
